---
title: "BEE_Toddler Feasibility"
author: "Jenna Frey"
date: "started 2025-02-07"
output: html_document
---

# FILE SET UP 
## a. Loading libraries and data import
```{r}

# Load packages (list from Elise)
library(openxlsx) # for reading and writing out files
library(table1) # for making tables
library(tidyverse) # for data organization and manipulation
library(ggpubr)  # plots
library(rstatix) # tukeys
library(ltm) # cronbach's alpha
library(FSA) # dunns test
library(patchwork) # for combining graphs
library(ggtext)
library(compareGroups)
# for random forest
library(lubridate)
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(e1071)
library(ggsci)
library(themis)
library(this.path)


select <- dplyr::select
rename <- dplyr::rename

# set working directory 
setwd(this.dir())

# main data import
data <- read.csv("1_Data/2025_1217_BEE_toddlerband_feasibility.csv")

# qualitative additions from the band diary 
issues <- read.xlsx("1_Data/QualCode.xlsx")

```

## b. Filtering only BEE participants who participated in the band activity
```{r}

bands <- data %>% 
  filter(bwb_diaryret_T30==1)
  
```

## c. Exploring missingness of the outcome (wear time)
```{r}

# of participants who completed the band visit, who did not return a wrist band?
bands %>% count(bwb_cwret_T30)
#10

# of participants who completed the band visit, who did not return an ankle band?
bands %>% count(bwb_caret_T30)
# 10, 9(==0) and 1(NA)

# of participants who completed the band visit, who did not return both?
bands %>% count(bwb_caret_T30==0 & bwb_cwret_T30==0)
# 6 did not return both bands, 1 had NA responses for both wrist and ankle

# what is the missingness in entire cohort?
all_na <- c(sapply(data, function(x) sum(is.na(x))))
all_vars <- c(names(data))
all_na <- data.frame(all_vars, all_na)

# what is the missingness only among band participants?
band_na <- c(sapply(bands, function(x) sum(is.na(x))))
band_vars <- c(names(bands))
band_na <- data.frame(band_vars, band_na)

# what is the missingness by participant?
bands_indiv_na <- bands %>% 
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(var_na=sum(is.na(c_across(bwb_cwret_T30:bwb_pwhrsnitetime7_T30)))) %>% 
  select(S_ID, var_na)

# participant 1023 has missing values for 83 of total columns

# removal of participants who opted out or were missing >75% of data
bands <- bands %>% 
  filter(!S_ID== 1015, # child opted out
         !S_ID== 1023, # missingness shown above
         !S_ID== 1097) # child opted out

# this give a baseline analytical sample size of n=115

```

## d. Setting up dfs
###  1. totals
###  2. separate dfs for each person/band position
###  3. attributes and wear time 
```{r}
# for total, day, and night wear over the week 
wksumwear <- bands %>% 
  select(S_ID, bwb_cwhrsweek_T30, bwb_cwhrsweekday_T30, bwb_cwhrsweeknite_T30, bwb_cahrsweek_T30, bwb_cahrsweekday_T30, bwb_cahrsweeknite_T30, bwb_pwhrsweek_T30, bwb_pwhrsweekday_T30,
bwb_pwhrsweeknite_T30) %>% 
  rename("child_wrist_wktotal" ="bwb_cwhrsweek_T30",
         "child_wrist_daytotal" = "bwb_cwhrsweekday_T30",
         "child_wrist_nighttotal" = "bwb_cwhrsweeknite_T30",
         "child_ankle_wktotal" = "bwb_cahrsweek_T30",
         "child_ankle_daytotal" = "bwb_cahrsweekday_T30",
         "child_ankle_nighttotal" = "bwb_cahrsweeknite_T30",
         "parent_parentwrist_wktotal" = "bwb_pwhrsweek_T30",
         "parent_parentwrist_daytotal" = "bwb_pwhrsweekday_T30", 
         "parent_parentwrist_nighttotal" = "bwb_pwhrsweeknite_T30") %>% 
  pivot_longer(-S_ID, names_to="longname", values_to="hours") %>% 
  separate(longname, c("person", "position", "day"), sep="_")

# separate dfs by person (child, adult) and position (wrist,ankle)
wksum_child_wrist <- wksumwear %>% 
  filter(person == "child", position == "wrist")

wksum_child_ankle <- wksumwear %>% 
  filter(person == "child", position == "ankle")

wksum_adult <- wksumwear %>% 
  filter(person == "parent")


# df of child attributes and weartime totals
attributes_weartimetotals <- bands %>% 
  rename("child_wrist_wktotal" = "bwb_cwhrsweek_T30",
       "child_wrist_daytotal" = "bwb_cwhrsweekday_T30",
       "child_wrist_nighttotal" = "bwb_cwhrsweeknite_T30",
       "child_ankle_wktotal" = "bwb_cahrsweek_T30",
       "child_ankle_daytotal" = "bwb_cahrsweekday_T30",
       "child_ankle_nighttotal" = "bwb_cahrsweeknite_T30",
       "parent_parentwrist_wktotal" = "bwb_pwhrsweek_T30",
       "parent_parentwrist_daytotal" = "bwb_pwhrsweekday_T30", 
       "parent_parentwrist_nighttotal" = "bwb_pwhrsweeknite_T30") %>% 
  select(S_ID, PSI_Mn_T30, PSI_DC_T30, MCDI3_All_T30, ECBQ_dis_T30, ECBQ_EFFCO_T30, ECBQ_NEGAF_T30, ECBQ_SURGE_T30, BIQ_Soc_T30, BIQ_Nov_T30, BIQ_Tot_T30, PAS_AVG_T30, child_wrist_wktotal, child_wrist_daytotal, child_ankle_wktotal, child_ankle_daytotal, child_ankle_nighttotal, parent_parentwrist_wktotal, parent_parentwrist_daytotal, parent_parentwrist_nighttotal) 

```

## e. Exploring missingness (attributes)
```{r}
#wear time and attribute data for ankle
wk_child_ankle_attributes <- merge(wksum_child_ankle, attributes_weartimetotals, by="S_ID", all.x= TRUE) 

#wear time and attribute data for wrist
wk_child_wrist_attributes <- merge(wksum_child_wrist, attributes_weartimetotals, by="S_ID", all.x= TRUE) 

wk_adult_attributes <- merge(wksum_adult, attributes_weartimetotals, by="S_ID", all.x= TRUE)

#attributes only
attributes <- wk_child_ankle_attributes %>% 
  select(S_ID, PSI_Mn_T30:PAS_AVG_T30)

#each participant was in the df 3 times
attributes <- distinct(attributes)

# missingness by attribute
attribute_na <- c(sapply(attributes, function(x) sum(is.na(x))))
attribute_vars <- c(names(attributes))
attribute_na <- data.frame(attribute_vars, attribute_na) %>% 
  rename("attribute"="attribute_na")

# missingness by person
attributes_indiv_na <- attributes %>% 
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(var_na=sum(is.na(c_across(PSI_Mn_T30:PAS_AVG_T30)))) %>% 
  select(S_ID, var_na)

  # 12 participants are missing all attributes

```

## f. Evaluating distributions / normality of attributes
### -Histograms
### -Shapiro Wilkes for normality
### -QQ plots
```{r}

###### VISUAL ASSESSMENT

# histograms
attributes_long <- bands %>% 
  select(S_ID, PSI_Mn_T30, PSI_DC_T30, MCDI3_All_T30, ECBQ_dis_T30, ECBQ_EFFCO_T30, ECBQ_NEGAF_T30, ECBQ_SURGE_T30, BIQ_Soc_T30, BIQ_Nov_T30, BIQ_Tot_T30, PAS_AVG_T30,) %>% 
  pivot_longer(!S_ID, names_to="variable", values_to= "value")


attribute_hist <- ggplot(attributes_long, aes(value)) + 
  geom_histogram(fill="cornflowerblue", color= "black", binwidth= function(x) {(max(x)-min(x))/25}) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales= "free") +
  labs( y = "Number of Observations", x= "Value", title= "Distributions of Child Attributes") 

attribute_hist

# qqplot 
attribute_qqplot <- ggqqplot(attributes_long, x= "value", facet.by= "variable", ggtheme= theme_bw(), scales= "free")

attribute_qqplot

###### QUANTITATIVE ASSESSMENT

#shapiro wilkes test  
shapiro_attributes <- apply(attributes_weartimetotals %>% select(-S_ID), 2, shapiro.test)

shapiro_attributes <- do.call(rbind.data.frame, shapiro_attributes)

shapiro_attributes <- shapiro_attributes %>% 
  mutate(normal = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, F, T)) %>%
  select(c(p.value, normal)) 

shapiro_attributes
# normal distributions aside from PSI_DC, MCDI_ALL, ECBQ_Dis

```

## g. creating Table 1: Birthing parent and child demographic characteristics
```{r}

bands %>% count(tc_race)

# checked with the BEE team, and the child missing race data is White, so we will manually change the missing value here: 
bands <- bands %>% 
  mutate(tc_race=ifelse(is.na(tc_race), 0, tc_race))

bands %>% count(tc_race)



# Using same coding criteria as in Hickman et al. (2024) for demographic characteristics of the sample
clean_demo_data <- bands %>%
  select(S_ID:tc_race_other) %>%
  rename("child_age_months_t30" = "TC_AgeMonths_Home_T30", 
         "mat_age_t30" = "PC_matage_home_T30") %>%
  mutate(across(TC_Female:pc_ed, as.character)) %>%
  mutate(TC_Female = recode(TC_Female, 
                            "0" = "M", 
                            "1" = "F")) %>%
  rename("child_sex" = "TC_Female") %>%
  mutate(pc_latino = recode(pc_latino,
                            "0" = "No", 
                            "1" = "MexicanChicano", 
                            "2" = "PuertoRican", 
                            "3" = "Cuban", 
                            "4" = "Other"),
         pc_latino = case_when(pc_latino == "No" ~ "No", 
                               .default = "Yes"),
         tc_latino = recode(tc_latino, 
                            "0" = "No", 
                            "1" = "MexicanChicano", 
                            "2" = "PuertoRican", 
                            "3" = "Cuban", 
                            "4" = "Other"),
         tc_latino = case_when(tc_latino == "No" ~ "No", 
                               .default = "Yes"),
         pc_race = recode(pc_race, "0" = "White", 
                          "1" = "Black", 
                          "2" = "IndigenousAmerican", 
                          "3" = "AsianIndian", 
                          "4" = "Chinese", 
                          "5" = "Japanese", 
                          "6" = "Korean", 
                          "7" = "Vietnamese", 
                          "8" = "OtherAsian", 
                          "9" = "Other"),
         pc_biracial = recode(pc_biracial, "0" = "No", 
                              "1" = "Yes"),
         pc_addrace = recode(pc_addrace, "0" = "White", 
                             "1" = "Black", 
                             "2" = "IndigenousAmerican", 
                             "3" = "AsianIndian", 
                             "4" = "Chinese", 
                             "5" = "Japanese", 
                             "6" = "Korean", 
                             "7" = "Vietnamese", 
                             "8" = "OtherAsian", 
                             "9" = "Other"),
         pc_race_black = recode(pc_race_black, 
                                "0" = "No", 
                                "1" = "Yes"),
         pc_ed = recode(pc_ed, 
                        "11" = "High School, GED, or less",
                        "12" = "High School, GED, or less", 
                        "13" = "High School, GED, or less", 
                        "14" = "Associate Degree", 
                        "15" = "Associate Degree", 
                        "16" = "Four-Year Degree", 
                        "17" = "Four-Year Degree", 
                        "18" = "Master's Degree", 
                        "19" = "Professional Degree or PhD"),
         tc_race = recode(tc_race, 
                          "0" = "White", 
                          "1" = "Black", 
                          "2" = "IndigenousAmerican", 
                          "3" = "AsianIndian", 
                          "4" = "Chinese", 
                          "5" = "Japanese", 
                          "6" = "Korean", 
                          "7" = "Vietnamese", 
                          "8" = "OtherAsian", 
                          "9" = "Other"),
         tc_biracial = recode(tc_biracial, 
                              "0" = "No", 
                              "1" = "Yes"),
         tc_addrace = recode(tc_addrace, 
                             "0" = "White", 
                             "1" = "Black", 
                             "2" = "IndigenousAmerican", 
                             "3" = "AsianIndian", 
                             "4" = "Chinese", 
                             "5" = "Japanese", 
                             "6" = "Korean", 
                             "7" = "Vietnamese", 
                             "8" = "OtherAsian", 
                             "9" = "Other"),
         tc_race_black = recode(tc_race_black, 
                                "0" = "No", 
                                "1" = "Yes")
         ) %>%
  select(-c(pc_race_black, tc_race_black)) %>%
  mutate(pc_latino_hispanic = case_when(pc_latino == "Yes" ~ "Yes",
                                        pc_race_other == "Hispanic" ~ "Yes",
                                        pc_race_other == "Mexican" ~ "Yes",
                                        pc_race_other == "Latina" ~ "Yes",
                                        .default = "No"),
         pc_multiracial_cleaned = case_when(pc_race_other == "biracial" ~ "Yes",
                                            pc_race_other == "multi" ~ "Yes",
                                            pc_biracial == "Yes" ~ "Yes",
                                            .default = "No"),
         pc_race_cleaned = case_when(pc_multiracial_cleaned == "Yes" ~ "Biracial/Multiracial",
                                     pc_race == "White" ~ "White",
                                     pc_race == "Black" ~ "Black",
                                     pc_race == "IndigenousAmerican" ~ "Other", 
                                     pc_race == "AsianIndian" ~ "Other",
                                     pc_race == "Chinese" ~ "Other",
                                     pc_race == "Japanese" ~ "Other", 
                                     pc_race == "Korean" ~ "Other",
                                     pc_race == "Vietnamese" ~ "Other",
                                     pc_race =="OtherAsian" ~ "Other",
                                     pc_race == "Other" ~ "Other")) %>%
  mutate(tc_latino_hispanic = case_when(tc_latino == "Yes" ~ "Yes",
                                        tc_race_other == "Hispanic" ~ "Yes",
                                        tc_race_other == "Mexican" ~ "Yes",
                                        tc_race_other == "Latina" ~ "Yes",
                                        .default = "No"),
         tc_multiracial_cleaned = case_when(tc_race_other == "biracial" ~ "Yes",
                                            tc_race_other == "multi" ~ "Yes",
                                            tc_biracial == "Yes" ~ "Yes",
                                            .default = "No"),
         tc_race_cleaned = case_when(tc_multiracial_cleaned == "Yes" ~ "Biracial/Multiracial",
                                     tc_race == "White" ~ "White",
                                     tc_race == "Black" ~ "Black",
                                     tc_race == "Chinese" ~ "Other",
                                     tc_race == "Other" ~ "Other")) %>%
  select(-c(pc_latino:pc_addrace, pc_race_other, last_pc_ed:pc_race_other, pc_multiracial_cleaned, tc_latino:tc_race_other, tc_multiracial_cleaned))

#from EH 7/18
# Split demographics table into child and mother tables, rename columns so they match, and create grouping column to indicate whether data go with mother or child
clean_demo_data_tc <- clean_demo_data %>%
  select(c(S_ID, child_age_months_t30, child_sex, tc_latino_hispanic, tc_race_cleaned)) %>%
  rename("age" = "child_age_months_t30", "sex" = "child_sex", "latino_hispanic" = "tc_latino_hispanic", "race" = "tc_race_cleaned") %>%
  mutate(group = "Child") %>%
  mutate(education = NA)

clean_demo_data_pc <- clean_demo_data %>%
  select(c(S_ID, mat_age_t30, pc_ed, pc_latino_hispanic, pc_race_cleaned)) %>%
  rename("age" = "mat_age_t30", "latino_hispanic" = "pc_latino_hispanic", "race" = "pc_race_cleaned", "education" = "pc_ed") %>%
  mutate(sex = "F", group = "Parent")

# Merge together and level factors
clean_demo_data_long <- bind_rows(clean_demo_data_pc, clean_demo_data_tc) %>%
  mutate(race = factor(race, levels = c("White", "Black", "Biracial/Multiracial", "Other")),
         latino_hispanic = factor(latino_hispanic, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
         education = factor(education, levels=c("High School, GED, or less", "Associate Degree", 
                                                "Four-Year Degree", "Master's Degree","Professional Degree or PhD")),
         sex = factor(sex, levels = c("F", "M")),
         group = factor(group, levels = c("Parent", "Child")))

# Add labels
label(clean_demo_data_long$age) <- "Age"
label(clean_demo_data_long$education) <- "Education"
label(clean_demo_data_long$latino_hispanic) <- "Latino or Hispanic"
label(clean_demo_data_long$race) <- "Race"
label(clean_demo_data_long$sex) <- "Sex"

# Create function for custom table so that Mean (SD) is shown for continuous variables
my.render.cont <- function(x) {
  with(stats.apply.rounding(stats.default(x), digits=2), c("",
                                                           "Mean (SD)"=sprintf("%s (&plusmn; %s)", MEAN, SD)))
}

# Make table of demographics
table1(~ sex + age + race + latino_hispanic + education | group,
       data = clean_demo_data_long, 
       render.continuous = my.render.cont, 
       overall = FALSE)

```



## first half of this code chunk written by allison spring to compare those who are in our analytical sample to those who were not 
```{r}

data %>% count(Complete_Home_T30)

#These are the S_IDs of the 115 
my_list<-bands$S_ID

included_subjects <- data[data$S_ID %in% my_list, ] %>%
  dplyr::filter(Complete_Home_T30==1)


Removed_subjects <- data[!data$S_ID %in% my_list, ] %>%
  filter(Complete_Home_T30==1)


included_subjects <- lapply(included_subjects, as.numeric)
Removed_subjects  <- lapply(Removed_subjects, as.numeric)

Removed_subjects <- as.data.frame(Removed_subjects)
included_subjects <- as.data.frame(included_subjects)

# Identify numeric columns (skip non-numeric)
numeric_cols <- sapply(Removed_subjects, is.numeric)

# Subset the data frame to include only numeric columns
df_numeric <- Removed_subjects[, numeric_cols]

# Compute the column means for only numeric columns
averages <- colMeans(df_numeric, na.rm = TRUE)

# Print the result
print(averages)

# Compute the average of each column in both datasets
averages_included_subjects <- colMeans(included_subjects, na.rm = TRUE)
averages_Removed_subjects <- colMeans(Removed_subjects, na.rm = TRUE)

# Combine the averages into a data frame for better visualization
averages_combined <- data.frame(
  averages_included_subjects = averages_included_subjects,
  averages_Removed_subjects = averages_Removed_subjects
)

# Print the averages
print(averages_combined)


# jcf
Removed_subjects$in_sample <- "0"
included_subjects$in_sample <- "1"

all_subjects <- rbind(Removed_subjects, included_subjects)
all_subjects <- all_subjects %>% 
  mutate(across(TC_Female:tc_race_other, as.factor))

# comparison using compareGroups
table1_comparison <- compareGroups(in_sample ~ . - S_ID, data= all_subjects, method=4)
  # method = 4 does a shapiro wilks test to determine normality of each var

table1_comparison

createTable(table1_comparison)

hist(all_subjects$income2needs_T30)
wilcox.test(income2needs_T30 ~in_sample, data=all_subjects, alternative="two.sided")

```





# RQ1: HOW COMPLIANT WERE TODDLERS WITH WEARING THE SILICONE WRISTBAND AND ANKLE BAND (AS DOCUMENTED BY THE PARENT-COMPLETED DAILY DIARIES)?

## a. Histograms, univariate distributions, and summary statistics (mean, median, min, max, std) for wear time by person (child, parent) and band position (wrist, ankle)
```{r}
###### VISUAL ASSESSMENT: histograms

# CHILD WRIST
#day
hist(bands$bwb_cwhrsweekday_T30)

#night
hist(bands$bwb_cwhrsweeknite_T30)

#total 
hist(bands$bwb_cwhrsweek_T30)

# CHILD ANKLE 
#day
hist(bands$bwb_cahrsweekday_T30)

#night
hist(bands$bwb_cahrsweeknite_T30)

#total 
hist(bands$bwb_cahrsweek_T30)

# PARENT WRIST
#day
hist(bands$bwb_pwhrsweekday_T30)

#night
hist(bands$bwb_pwhrsweeknite_T30)

#total 
hist(bands$bwb_pwhrsweek_T30)

```

```{r}
##### QUANTITATIVE ASSESSMENT

# summary statistics for each person (child, parent), band position (wrist, ankle), and time (day total [max 84], night total [max 84], week total [max 168])
sumstat_totalweekwear <- wksumwear %>% 
  group_by(day, person, position) %>% 
  summarise(median=median(hours, na.rm=TRUE), min= min(hours, na.rm=TRUE), max=max(hours, na.rm=TRUE), mean=mean(hours, na.rm= TRUE), std= sd(hours, na.rm=TRUE))

sumstat_totalweekwear

## comparison of wrist and ankle wear time overall and within time of day (day, night) strata ##

# creating a new df 
wksumsep <- wksumwear %>% 
  filter(!(day=="wktotal"),                                              # only day and night wear
         !(person=="parent")) %>%                                        # only children
  mutate(day = case_when(day == "daytotal" ~ "Day",                      # cleaning of var names for figure 1 later
                        day == "nighttotal" ~ "Night")) %>% 
  mutate(position = case_when(position == "wrist" ~ "Child Wrist",
                        position == "ankle" ~ "Child Ankle",
                        position == "parentwrist" ~ "Parent Wrist"))

## OVERALL COMPARISON 

# unpaired comparison of hours worn between the wrist and ankle band overall (day and night together)
wilcox.test(hours ~ position, data=wksumsep)

# paired comparison of hours worn between the wrist and ankle band overall (day and night together)
wilcox.test(x = wksumsep$hours[wksumsep$position == "Child Wrist"],  
            y = wksumsep$hours[wksumsep$position == "Child Ankle"],  
            paired = TRUE)


## WITHIN TIME OF DAY STRATA 

# DAYTIME

# new df of only day
wksumsep_day <- wksumsep %>% 
  filter(day=="Day")

# unpaired comparison of hours worn between the wrist and ankle band during the day
wilcox.test(hours ~ position, data=wksumsep_day)

# paired comparison of hours worn between the wrist and ankle band during the day
wilcox.test(x = wksumsep_day$hours[wksumsep_day$position == "Child Wrist"],  
            y = wksumsep_day$hours[wksumsep_day$position == "Child Ankle"],  
            paired = TRUE)



# NIGHTTIME

# new df of only night 
wksumsep_night <- wksumsep %>% 
  filter(day=="Night")

# unpaired comparison of hours worn between the wrist and ankle band at night
wilcox.test(hours ~ position, data=wksumsep_night)

# paired comparison of hours worn between the wrist and ankle band at night 
wilcox.test(x = wksumsep_night$hours[wksumsep_night$position == "Child Wrist"],  
            y = wksumsep_night$hours[wksumsep_night$position == "Child Ankle"],  
            paired = TRUE)





# Within wrist band, is wear time different by time of day?
wksumsep_child_wrist <- wksum_child_wrist %>% 
  filter(!day=="wktotal")

# unpaired comparison of hours worn between the day and night for the wrist band
wilcox.test(hours ~ day, data=wksumsep_child_wrist)

# paired comparison of hours worn between the day and night for the wrist band
wilcox.test(x = wksumsep_child_wrist$hours[wksumsep_child_wrist$day == "daytotal"],  
            y = wksumsep_child_wrist$hours[wksumsep_child_wrist$day == "nighttotal"],  
            paired = TRUE)


# Within ankle band, is wear time different by time of day?
wksumsep_child_ankle <- wksum_child_ankle %>% 
  filter(!day=="wktotal")

# paired comparison of hours worn between the day and night for the ankle band
wilcox.test(hours ~ day, data=wksumsep_child_ankle)

# paired comparison of hours worn between the day and night for the ankle band
wilcox.test(x = wksumsep_child_ankle$hours[wksumsep_child_ankle$day == "daytotal"],  
            y = wksumsep_child_ankle$hours[wksumsep_child_ankle$day == "nighttotal"],  
            paired = TRUE)

```
### Primary focus is on DAYTIME WEAR ONLY
## -new dataframes of only day time wear by each day (1-7) 
## -summary statistics of day time wear
## -correlation between number of days worn and total week wear time
```{r}
# new long df of day time wear by each day with cleaned variable names
daywear <- bands %>% 
  select(S_ID, contains("daytime")) %>% 
  rename("child_wrist_1" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime1_T30",                      
         "child_ankle_1" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime1_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_1"= "bwb_pwhrsdaytime1_T30",
         "child_wrist_2" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime2_T30",
         "child_ankle_2" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime2_T30",
         "parent_wrist_2" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime2_T30",
         "child_wrist_3" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime3_T30", 
         "child_ankle_3" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime3_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_3" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime3_T30",
         "child_wrist_4" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime4_T30", 
         "child_ankle_4" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime4_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_4" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime4_T30",
         "child_wrist_5" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime5_T30", 
         "child_ankle_5" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime5_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_5" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime5_T30",
         "child_wrist_6" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime6_T30", 
         "child_ankle_6" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime6_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_6" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime6_T30",
         "child_wrist_7" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime7_T30", 
         "child_ankle_7" ="bwb_cahrsdaytime7_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_7" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime7_T30") %>% 
  pivot_longer(-S_ID, names_to="longname", values_to="hours") %>% 
  separate(longname, c("person", "position", "day"), sep="_")

# summary statistics for daywear data (day by day)
    ## median, minimum, maximum, mean, and standard deviation
sumstat_day <- daywear %>% 
  group_by(day, person, position) %>% 
  summarise(median=median(hours, na.rm=TRUE), min= min(hours, na.rm=TRUE), max=max(hours, na.rm=TRUE), mean=mean(hours, na.rm= TRUE), std= sd(hours, na.rm=TRUE))



# counts of the days by band/person with missing values
daywear %>% group_by(day, person, position) %>% count(is.na(hours))
#day 1,2,3,6 no missing 
#day 4 missing reported hours for 1 child wrist, 1 child ankle, and 1 parent wrist band (all same family--1153)
#day 5 missing reported hours for 2 child wrist, 2 child ankle, and 2 parent wrist band (all same families-- 1153, 1059)
#day 7 missing reported hours for 3 child wrist, 3 child ankle, and 3 parent wrist band (all same families-- 1153, 1116, 1029)


# wide df of day time wear for each participant by day with counts of how many times they fall in each of the following categories across the week (max 7)
  # 0-1 hours    = low 
  # 2-10 hours   = mid
  # 11-12 hours  = high
daywear_short <- daywear %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from= day, values_from= hours) %>% 
   rename(day1="1", 
         day2="2",
         day3="3",
         day4="4",
         day5="5",
         day6="6", 
         day7="7") %>%  
  mutate(n.na=rowSums(is.na(.)), 
         n.0_1=rowSums(select(.,c(day1:day7)) < 2, na.rm=TRUE),
         n.2_10=rowSums((select(.,c(day1:day7))) < 11 & (select(.,c(day1:day7))) > 1, na.rm=TRUE), 
         n.11_12=rowSums(select(.,c(day1:day7)) > 10, na.rm=TRUE), 
         n.2_12=rowSums(select(.,c(day1:day7)) > 1, na.rm=TRUE)) %>% 
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(n.total=sum(c_across(c(n.na:n.11_12)))) %>% #check
  ungroup()

# creating a new binary variable of worn/not worn for the day where
## worn= 2 or more hours a day 
## not worn= less than 2 hours a day 
daywear_short_binary <- daywear_short %>% 
  mutate(day1b=case_when(day1== NA ~ NA, 
                         day1 < 2 ~ 0,
                         day1 >= 2 ~ 1),
         day2b=case_when(day2== NA ~ NA, 
                         day2 < 2 ~ 0,
                         day2 >= 2 ~ 1), 
         day3b=case_when(day3== NA ~ NA, 
                         day3 < 2 ~ 0,
                         day3 >= 2 ~ 1), 
         day4b=case_when(day4== NA ~ NA, 
                         day4 < 2 ~ 0,
                         day4 >= 2 ~ 1), 
         day5b=case_when(day5== NA ~ NA, 
                         day5 < 2 ~ 0,
                         day5 >= 2 ~ 1), 
         day6b=case_when(day6== NA ~ NA, 
                         day6 < 2 ~ 0,
                         day6 >= 2 ~ 1), 
         day7b=case_when(day7== NA ~ NA, 
                         day7 < 2 ~ 0,
                         day7 >= 2 ~ 1)) %>% 
  mutate(n.daysworn=rowSums(select(.,c(day1b:day7b)), na.rm=TRUE))

## 8 hour threshold 
daywear_short_binary <- daywear_short_binary %>% 
  mutate(day1_8=case_when(day1 == NA ~ NA, 
                          day1 <  8 ~ 0,
                          day1 >= 8 ~ 1),
         day2_8=case_when(day2 == NA ~ NA, 
                          day2 <  8 ~ 0,
                          day2 >= 8 ~ 1), 
         day3_8=case_when(day3 == NA ~ NA, 
                          day3 <  8 ~ 0,
                          day3 >= 8 ~ 1), 
         day4_8=case_when(day4 == NA ~ NA, 
                          day4 <  8 ~ 0,
                          day4 >= 8 ~ 1), 
         day5_8=case_when(day5 == NA ~ NA, 
                          day5 <  8 ~ 0,
                          day5 >= 8 ~ 1), 
         day6_8=case_when(day6 == NA ~ NA, 
                          day6 <  8 ~ 0,
                          day6 >= 8 ~ 1), 
         day7_8=case_when(day7 == NA ~ NA, 
                         day7 <  8 ~ 0,
                         day7 >= 8 ~ 1)) %>% 
  mutate(n.daysworn_8=rowSums(select(.,c(day1_8:day7_8)), na.rm=TRUE))








# selecting only the daytime weekly wear time total variable for ankle and wrist from "bands" df in order to merge it with the "daywear_short_binary" df
bands_child_daytotals <- bands %>% 
  select(S_ID, bwb_cwhrsweekday_T30, bwb_cahrsweekday_T30) %>% 
  rename("child_wrist_daytotal" = "bwb_cwhrsweekday_T30", 
         "child_ankle_daytotal" = "bwb_cahrsweekday_T30")

# merge
daywear_short_binary <- merge(daywear_short_binary, bands_child_daytotals, all=TRUE)

# visual assessment of normality 
## non normal, will proceed with a spearman test for correlations
hist(daywear_short_binary$n.daysworn)
hist(daywear_short_binary$child_wrist_daytotal)
hist(daywear_short_binary$child_ankle_daytotal)

daywear_short_binary_ankle <- daywear_short_binary %>% 
  filter(person=="child", 
         position=="ankle")

daywear_short_binary_wrist <- daywear_short_binary %>% 
  filter(person=="child", 
         position=="wrist")

# is the number of days worn in a week correlated with the total hours worn over the week for the ankle band?
cor.test(daywear_short_binary_ankle$n.daysworn, daywear_short_binary_ankle$child_ankle_daytotal, method="spearman")

# plot
daywear_corr_ankle <- ggplot(daywear_short_binary_ankle, aes(x=n.daysworn, y=child_ankle_daytotal, color=position)) + 
  geom_point(alpha= 0.7, size=3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = ("ankle" = "cornflowerblue")) +
  stat_cor(method= "spearman", label.x = 1, label.y = 80, color= "black") +
  geom_point()

daywear_corr_ankle

# is the number of days worn in a week correlated with the total hours worn over the week for the wrist band?
cor.test(daywear_short_binary_wrist$n.daysworn, daywear_short_binary_wrist$child_wrist_daytotal, method="spearman")

# plot
daywear_corr_wrist <- ggplot(daywear_short_binary_wrist, aes(x=n.daysworn, y=child_wrist_daytotal, color=position)) + 
  geom_point(alpha= 0.7, size=3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = ("wrist" = "orange")) +
  stat_cor(method= "spearman", label.x = 1, label.y = 80, color= "black") 

daywear_corr_wrist

# combined plot of wrist/ankle
corr_by_day <- daywear_corr_ankle + daywear_corr_wrist +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"))
  
corr_by_day

# jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/corr_by_day.jpeg",
#     width = 8, height = 4, units="in", res=1200)
# corr_by_day
# invisible(dev.off())



# ankle only min and max for total hours across the week
daywear_short_sum_ankle <- daywear_short_binary_ankle %>% 
  group_by(n.daysworn) %>% 
  summarise(min_ankle=min(child_ankle_daytotal), max_ankle=max(child_ankle_daytotal))

# wrist only min and max for total hours across the week
daywear_short_sum_wrist <- daywear_short_binary_wrist %>% 
  group_by(n.daysworn) %>% 
  summarise(min_wrist=min(child_wrist_daytotal), max_wrist=max(child_wrist_daytotal))

# number of participants who wore the band for 0-7 days for the wrist and ankle bands
daywear_short_binary_wrist %>% count(n.daysworn)
daywear_short_binary_ankle %>% count(n.daysworn)

# combined df of min and max of total hours worn in the week by the number of days worn
daywear_short_sum <- merge(daywear_short_sum_ankle, daywear_short_sum_wrist)

daywear_short_sum




# ankle only min and max for total hours across the week
daywear_short_sum_ankle8 <- daywear_short_binary_ankle %>% 
  group_by(n.daysworn_8) %>% 
  summarise(min_ankle=min(child_ankle_daytotal), max_ankle=max(child_ankle_daytotal))

# wrist only min and max for total hours across the week
daywear_short_sum_wrist8 <- daywear_short_binary_wrist %>% 
  group_by(n.daysworn_8) %>% 
  summarise(min_wrist=min(child_wrist_daytotal), max_wrist=max(child_wrist_daytotal))

# number of participants who wore the band for 0-7 days for the wrist and ankle bands
daywear_short_binary_wrist %>% count(n.daysworn_8)
daywear_short_binary_ankle %>% count(n.daysworn_8)

# combined df of min and max of total hours worn in the week by the number of days worn
daywear_short_sum_8 <- merge(daywear_short_sum_ankle8, daywear_short_sum_wrist8)

daywear_short_sum_8


```

## b. reliability across the week (cronbach's alpha)
```{r}
#dfs by ankle and wrist for day 1 through 7 
daywear_days1_7_ankle <- daywear_short %>% 
  filter(position=="ankle") %>% 
  select(day1:day7) 
 
daywear_days1_7_wrist <- daywear_short %>% 
  filter(position=="wrist", 
         person=="child") %>% 
  select(day1:day7) 
  
#dfs by ankle and wrist for day 2 through 7
daywear_days2_7_ankle <- daywear_days1_7_ankle %>% 
  select(-day1) 

daywear_days2_7_wrist <- daywear_days1_7_wrist %>% 
  select(-day1)

#ankle reliability test (cronbach's alpha)

#days 1 through 7
cronbach.alpha(daywear_days1_7_ankle, CI=TRUE, na.rm=TRUE)
#days 2 through 7
cronbach.alpha(daywear_days2_7_ankle, CI=TRUE, na.rm=TRUE)

#wrist reliability test (cronbach's alpha)

#days 1 through 7
cronbach.alpha(daywear_days1_7_wrist, CI=TRUE, na.rm=TRUE)
#days 2 through 7
cronbach.alpha(daywear_days2_7_wrist, CI=TRUE, na.rm=TRUE)

```
## c. wear time distributions
### -justification of split at 72 hrs (6 days) based on the histogram
### -creating new binary variable at 72 hours
### -Spearman correlations and scatter plots for
####  1. child wrist vs. child ankle 
####  2. child wrist vs. parent wrist 
####  3. child ankle vs. parent wrist
```{r}

# combining ankle and wrist dfs that have attribute and day, night, and total hours for ONLY the child
wk_child_attributes <- bind_rows(wk_child_ankle_attributes, wk_child_wrist_attributes)

# combining ankle and wrist dfs that have attribute and day, night, and total hours for child and parent
wk_all_attributes <- bind_rows(wk_child_ankle_attributes, wk_child_wrist_attributes, wk_adult_attributes)

# selecting only daytime totals (focus of this analysis) and the child attributes
wk_child_day <- wk_child_attributes %>% 
  filter(person == "child" & day== "daytotal") %>% 
  select(-day,
         -person, 
         -contains("total"))

hist(wk_child_day$hours, breaks=20) # shows a split at 72 hours which is 6 days of wear

# new binary variable at 72 hours
wk_child_day <- wk_child_day %>% 
  mutate(cat_hours = case_when(hours >= 72 ~ "72 or more hours", 
                               hours < 72 ~ "< 72 hours"))

# check
wk_child_day %>% count(cat_hours)

# reorder variables in df
wk_child_day <- wk_child_day %>% 
  relocate(cat_hours, .before=PSI_Mn_T30)

# long df to plot 
long_wk_child_day <- wk_child_day %>% 
  select(-contains("total")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(!c(S_ID, position, cat_hours, hours), names_to = "attribute", values_to = "value")


# scatterplot of day time week long wear (y) for each attribute (scale on the x) by child wrist (orange) and ankle band (blue)
attribute_scat <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day, aes(x=value, y=hours, color=position, alpha= 0.5)) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("wrist" = "orange", 
                               "ankle" = "cornflowerblue")) + 
  geom_point() + 
  facet_wrap(~attribute, scales= "free")

attribute_scat

# df of adult hours with attributes to assess whether child wear time and parent wear time are correlated
wk_adult_day <- wk_all_attributes %>% 
  filter(person == "parent" & day== "daytotal") %>% 
  select(-day,
         -person)

# df of adult hours and SID only
wk_adult_day_hours <- wk_adult_day %>% 
  select(S_ID, hours) %>% 
  rename("adult_hours" = "hours")

# separating wrist and ankle to rename/create a new dataframe

# ankle only 
wk_child_day_ankle <- wk_child_day %>% 
  filter(position == "ankle")

wk_child_day_ankle_hours <- wk_child_day_ankle %>% 
  select(S_ID, hours, cat_hours) %>% 
  rename("ankle_hours" = "hours", 
         "ankle_cat_hours" = "cat_hours")

#wrist only
wk_child_day_wrist <- wk_child_day %>% 
  filter(position == "wrist")

wk_child_day_wrist_hours <- wk_child_day_wrist %>% 
  select(S_ID, hours, cat_hours) %>% 
  rename("wrist_hours" = "hours",
         "wrist_cat_hours" = "cat_hours")

wk_child_day_hours <- merge(wk_child_day_ankle_hours, wk_child_day_wrist_hours, all= TRUE)

wk_all_day_hours <- merge(wk_child_day_hours, wk_adult_day_hours, all= TRUE)



# what is the average difference across participants for ankle vs. wrist bands?
wk_child_day_hours$difference <- wk_child_day_hours$ankle_hours-wk_child_day_hours$wrist_hours 

mean(wk_child_day_hours$difference)

# **** paired wilcox sign rank test is presented after demo_weartime df is created ****




# child wrist to ankle spearman correlation
cor.test(wk_child_day_hours$ankle_hours, wk_child_day_hours$wrist_hours, method="spearman") 

# parent wrist to child wrist spearman correlation 
cor.test(wk_all_day_hours$adult_hours, wk_all_day_hours$wrist_hours, method="spearman") 

# parent wrist to child ankle spearman correlation
cor.test(wk_all_day_hours$adult_hours, wk_all_day_hours$ankle_hours, method="spearman") 



#CORRESPONDING PLOTS 

#wrist by ankle wear time
anklebywrist_scatter <- ggplot(wk_child_day_hours, aes(x= wrist_hours, y=ankle_hours)) + 
  geom_point(size= 3) + 
  stat_cor(method= "spearman", label.x = 3, label.y = 30) + 
  labs(title= "Child Band Daytime Wear")  

anklebywrist_scatter

#wrist by parent wear time 
wristbyparent_scatter <- ggplot(wk_all_day_hours, aes(x= wrist_hours, y=adult_hours)) + 
  geom_point(size= 3, color="orange") + 
  stat_cor(method= "spearman", label.x = 3, label.y = 30) + 
  labs(title= "Child wrist by Adult wrist Band Daytime Wear")  

wristbyparent_scatter

#ankle by parent wear time
anklebyparent_scatter <- ggplot(wk_all_day_hours, aes(x= ankle_hours, y=adult_hours)) + 
  geom_point(size= 3, color="cornflowerblue") + 
  stat_cor(method= "spearman", label.x = 3, label.y = 30) + 
  labs(title= "Child ankle by Adult wrist Band Daytime Wear")  

anklebyparent_scatter

```

## d. Low, moderate, and high wear time categorization by median wear time 
### Table 2
```{r}

median_cat_weartime <-  daywear %>% 
  group_by(S_ID, position, person) %>% 
  summarise(median= median(hours, na.rm=TRUE)) %>%  # median wear time across the 7 days 
  # for missing individual days, missing days were removed when calculating the median wear time
      # 3 participants were missing 1 day 
      # 1 participant was missing 3 days 
  mutate(hourscat= case_when(median < 2 ~ "0-1",
                            median < 11 ~ "2-10",
                            median > 10 ~ "11-12"))
# check
median_cat_weartime %>% 
  group_by(position, person) %>% 
  count(hourscat) 

# separating by position to make the matrix
# ankle
median_cat_weartime_ankle <-  median_cat_weartime %>% 
  filter(!person=="parent", 
         position=="ankle") %>% 
  rename(ankle_hourscat= hourscat) %>% 
  ungroup(position) %>% 
  select(S_ID, ankle_hourscat)

# wrist
median_cat_weartime_wrist <-  median_cat_weartime %>% 
  filter(!person=="parent", 
         position=="wrist") %>% 
  rename(wrist_hourscat= hourscat) %>%
  ungroup(position) %>% 
  select(S_ID, wrist_hourscat)


median_cat_weartime_matrix <- merge(median_cat_weartime_ankle, median_cat_weartime_wrist, all= TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(combined_med = case_when(ankle_hourscat == "11-12" & wrist_hourscat == "11-12" ~ "high ankle & high wrist", 
                                ankle_hourscat == "11-12" & wrist_hourscat == "2-10" ~ "high ankle & med wrist",
                                ankle_hourscat == "11-12" & wrist_hourscat == "0-1" ~ "high ankle & no/low wrist",
                                ankle_hourscat == "2-10" & wrist_hourscat == "11-12" ~ "med ankle & high wrist",
                                ankle_hourscat == "2-10" & wrist_hourscat == "2-10" ~ "med ankle & med wrist", 
                                ankle_hourscat == "2-10" & wrist_hourscat == "0-1" ~ "med ankle & no/low wrist",
                                ankle_hourscat == "0-1" & wrist_hourscat == "11-12" ~ "no/low ankle & high wrist",
                                ankle_hourscat == "0-1" & wrist_hourscat == "2-10" ~ "no/low ankle & med wrist", 
                                ankle_hourscat == "0-1" & wrist_hourscat == "0-1" ~ "no/low ankle & no/low wrist"))
median_cat_weartime_matrix %>% 
  count(combined_med)

```

## e. Low, moderate, and high wear time categorizations by total week wear
### Table 2
```{r}

# new categorical variable for consistency with the <6 days or ≥6 days of wear time over the week
totalhours_cat <- wk_child_day %>% 
  select(S_ID:hours) %>% 
  mutate(threecat_hours= case_when(hours < 12 ~ "low", #less than one full day
                              hours >= 12 & hours < 72 ~ "med", #mid 
                              hours >= 72 ~ "high")) #six or more days

totalhours_cat$position_cathours <- paste(totalhours_cat$position, totalhours_cat$threecat_hours)

# now need to position the dfs next to each other rather than stacked 
totalhours_cat_ankle <- totalhours_cat %>% 
  filter(position=="ankle") %>% 
  select(S_ID, position_cathours) %>% 
  rename("ankle_cathours"= "position_cathours")

totalhours_cat_wrist <- totalhours_cat %>% 
  filter(position=="wrist") %>% 
  select(S_ID, position_cathours) %>% 
  rename("wrist_cathours"= "position_cathours")

# only focused on about day x day and night x night not day x night cross for wrist/ankle
totalhours_cat_weartime_matrix <- merge(totalhours_cat_ankle, totalhours_cat_wrist, by="S_ID") %>%
  mutate(combined_total= case_when(wrist_cathours=="wrist high" & ankle_cathours=="ankle high" ~ "high wrist/ high ankle",
                              wrist_cathours=="wrist high" & ankle_cathours=="ankle med" ~ "high wrist/ med ankle",
                              wrist_cathours=="wrist high" & ankle_cathours=="ankle low" ~ "high wrist/ low ankle",
                              wrist_cathours=="wrist med" & ankle_cathours=="ankle high" ~ "med wrist/ high ankle",
                              wrist_cathours=="wrist med" & ankle_cathours=="ankle med" ~ "med wrist/ med ankle",
                              wrist_cathours=="wrist med" & ankle_cathours=="ankle low" ~ "med wrist/ low ankle",
                              wrist_cathours=="wrist low" & ankle_cathours=="ankle high" ~ "low wrist/ high ankle",
                              wrist_cathours=="wrist low" & ankle_cathours=="ankle med" ~ "low wrist/ med ankle",
                              wrist_cathours=="wrist low" & ankle_cathours=="ankle low" ~ "low wrist/ low ankle"))

totalhours_cat_weartime_matrix %>% count(combined_total)
```

# RQ2: TO WHAT EXTENT DO CHILD AND FAMILY CHARACTERISTICS PREDICT CHILD BAND COMPLIANCE?

## a. age and sex
```{r}

demo_weartime <- merge(wk_all_day_hours, totalhours_cat_weartime_matrix)
demo_weartime <- merge(demo_weartime,clean_demo_data_tc)

demo_weartime <- demo_weartime %>% 
  select(-education, # no child education
         -group)     # all children

demo_weartime <- demo_weartime %>% 
  mutate(ankle_cat6day=case_when(ankle_hours < 72 ~ "< 72 hours", 
                                 ankle_hours >= 72 ~ "72 or more hours"), 
         wrist_cat6day=case_when(wrist_hours < 72 ~ "< 72 hours", 
                                 wrist_hours >= 72 ~ "72 or more hours"))

# checks to make sure the categories match 
demo_weartime %>% count(ankle_cat6day)
demo_weartime %>% count(ankle_cathours)
demo_weartime %>% count(wrist_cat6day)
demo_weartime %>% count(wrist_cathours)

# AGE AND DAYTIME HOURS WORN

# plot of age (x) and wrist hours (y)
wristbyage_scatter <-  ggplot(demo_weartime, aes(x=age, y=wrist_hours)) + 
  geom_point(size= 3, color = "orange") + 
  labs(title= "Child Wrist Band Daytime Wear by Age") + 
 ylab("Wrist Daytime Wear") +
  xlab("Age (months)") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(30,30), label.y=c(35,40), digits= 3)

wristbyage_scatter

# plot of age (x) and ankle hours (y)
anklebyage_scatter <-  ggplot(demo_weartime, aes(x=age, y=ankle_hours)) + 
  geom_point(size= 3, color = "cornflowerblue") + 
  labs(title= "Child Ankle Band Daytime Wear by Age") + 
 ylab("Ankle Daytime Wear") +
  xlab("Age (months)") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(30,30), label.y=c(35,40), digits= 3)

anklebyage_scatter



# CHILD SEX AND DAYTIME HOURS WORN
## plots + wilcox rank sum (unpaired) test

# wrist
ggplot(demo_weartime, aes(x=sex, y=wrist_hours, fill= sex)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  labs(title= "Child Wrist Band Daytime Wear by Sex") + 
  theme(legend.position= "none") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("F" = "darkorange", 
                               "M" = "aquamarine3")) +
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.15)) + 
  stat_compare_means(method= "wilcox.test")

wilcox_test(data= demo_weartime, wrist_hours ~ sex)

# ankle
ggplot(demo_weartime, aes(x=sex, y=ankle_hours, fill= sex)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  labs(title= "Child Ankle Band Daytime Wear by Sex") + 
  theme(legend.position= "none") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("F" = "darkorange", 
                               "M" = "aquamarine3")) +
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.15)) + 
  stat_compare_means(method= "wilcox.test")

wilcox_test(data= demo_weartime, ankle_hours ~ sex)

# ankle by wrist band scatter (colors = sex)
anklebywrist_scatter_sex <-  ggplot(demo_weartime, aes(x=wrist_hours, y=ankle_hours, color= sex)) + 
  geom_point(size= 3) + 
  labs(title= "Child Band Daytime Wear by Sex") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("F" = "darkorange", 
                               "M" = "aquamarine3")) +
 ylab("Ankle Daytime Wear") +
  xlab("Wrist Daytime Wear") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(30,35))

anklebywrist_scatter_sex



# mean total wear time didn't differ by sex (boxplot/wilcox test) for wrist or ankle bands but for both male and female children, wrist and ankle wear time were correlated (scatter/spearman correlation)



# separate dfs of male and female
demo_weartime_female <- demo_weartime %>% 
  filter(sex=="F")

demo_weartime_male <- demo_weartime %>% 
  filter(sex=="M")


# female only plot
anklebywrist_scatter_f <-  ggplot(demo_weartime_female, aes(x=wrist_hours, y=ankle_hours)) + 
  geom_point(size= 3, color="darkorange") + 
  labs(title= "Child Band Daytime Wear for Female") +
  ylab("Ankle Daytime Wear") +
  xlab("Wrist Daytime Wear") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(30,35))

anklebywrist_scatter_f

# male only plot
anklebywrist_scatter_m <-  ggplot(demo_weartime_male, aes(x=wrist_hours, y=ankle_hours)) + 
  geom_point(size= 3, color="aquamarine3") + 
  labs(title= "Child Band Daytime Wear for Male") +
  ylab("Ankle Daytime Wear") +
  xlab("Wrist Daytime Wear") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(30,35), digits= 3)

anklebywrist_scatter_m



# AGE AND DAYTIME HOURS WORN STRATIFIED BY SEX

# ankle hours (y) by age (x) stratified by sex (M= teal, F= orange)
anklebyage_scatter_sex <-  ggplot(demo_weartime, aes(x=age, y=ankle_hours, color= sex)) + 
  geom_point(size= 3) + 
  labs(title= "Child Ankle Band Daytime Wear by Age (x) and Sex (color)") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("F" = "darkorange", 
                               "M" = "aquamarine3")) +
 ylab("Ankle Daytime Wear") +
  xlab("Age") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(30,30), label.y=c(35,40))

anklebyage_scatter_sex 

# wrist hours (y) by age (x) stratified by sex (M= teal, F= orange)
wristbyage_scatter_sex <-  ggplot(demo_weartime, aes(x=age, y=wrist_hours, color= sex)) + 
  geom_point(size= 3) + 
  labs(title= "Child Wrist Band Daytime Wear by Age (x) and Sex (color)") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("F" = "darkorange", 
                               "M" = "aquamarine3")) +
 ylab("Wrist Daytime Wear") +
  xlab("Age") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(30,30), label.y=c(35,40), digits= 3)

wristbyage_scatter_sex 



demo_weartime_ankle <- demo_weartime %>% 
  select(-wrist_hours, -wrist_cat_hours) %>% 
  mutate(position = "ankle") %>% 
  rename("hours"= "ankle_hours", 
         "cat6day" = "ankle_cat_hours")

demo_weartime_wrist <- demo_weartime %>% 
  select(-ankle_hours, -ankle_cat_hours) %>% 
  mutate(position = "wrist") %>% 
  rename("hours"= "wrist_hours", 
        "cat6day" = "wrist_cat_hours")

demo_weartime_sep <- rbind(demo_weartime_ankle, demo_weartime_wrist)
  


# by position, not paired
weartime_xband_yhours_groupedsex <-  ggplot(demo_weartime_sep, aes(x=position, y=hours, fill= sex)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) + 
  labs(title= "Child  Band Daytime Wear by Position and Child Sex", size= 6) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("F" = "darkorange", 
                               "M" = "aquamarine3")) +
  geom_jitter(size=0.3, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",  
                     label.x = 5, label.y = 90, size = 5)

weartime_xband_yhours_groupedsex


# unpaired
ggplot(demo_weartime_sep, aes(x=position, y=hours, fill= position)) + 
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) + 
  labs(title= "Child Band Daytime Wear by Position (unpaired)") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("wrist" = "orange", 
                               "ankle" = "cornflowerblue")) +
  geom_jitter(size=0.3, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                      label.y = 90, size = 5)

# paired, not stratified by sex
ggplot(demo_weartime_sep, aes(x=position, y=hours, fill= position)) + 
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) + 
  labs(title= "Child Band Daytime Wear by Position (paired)") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("wrist" = "orange", 
                               "ankle" = "cornflowerblue")) +
  geom_jitter(size=0.3, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test", paired= TRUE,
                      label.y = 90, size = 5)


# paired, stratified by sex
weartime_xsex_yhours_groupedband_paired <- ggplot(demo_weartime_sep, aes(x=sex, y=hours, fill= position)) + 
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) + 
  labs(title= "Child Band Daytime Wear by Sex (paired)") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("wrist" = "orange", 
                               "ankle" = "cornflowerblue")) +
  geom_jitter(size=0.3, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test", paired= TRUE,
                      label.x = 5, label.y = 90, size = 5)

weartime_xsex_yhours_groupedband_paired

# wilcox rank sum
wilcox.test(hours ~ position, data=demo_weartime_sep)
# wilcox sign rank 
wilcox.test(x = demo_weartime_sep$hours[demo_weartime_sep$position == "wrist"],  
            y = demo_weartime_sep$hours[demo_weartime_sep$position == "ankle"],  
            paired = TRUE)

# ** results presented under RQ1 results section manuscript **


# mean daytime wear hours for ankle and wrist
demo_weartime_sep %>% group_by(position) %>% summarise(mean(hours))

# mean daytime wear hours for ankle and wrist stratified by sex 
demo_weartime_sep %>% group_by(position, sex) %>% summarise(mean(hours))

```

## b. band wear time categories (total hours) and age
```{r}
# visual check for normality 
hist(demo_weartime$age)

# quant check for normality
shapiro.test(demo_weartime$age) # p= 0.06219, age is normally distributed, ok to use anova

demo_weartime$ankle_cathours <- factor(demo_weartime$ankle_cathours, 
                                    levels=c("ankle low", "ankle med", "ankle high"), ordered=TRUE)  

demo_weartime$wrist_cathours <- factor(demo_weartime$wrist_cathours, 
                                    levels=c("wrist low", "wrist med", "wrist high"), ordered=TRUE)

# ANOVA TO COMPARE THREE GROUPS (HIGH MED LOW)

# ankle band
ankle_anova <- aov(age ~ ankle_cathours,
            data= demo_weartime)
summary(ankle_anova)
    # no post hoc test needed

# wrist band
wrist_anova <- aov(age ~ wrist_cathours,
            data= demo_weartime) 
summary(wrist_anova)

# post hoc test for multiple comparisons
tukey_hsd(wrist_anova, conf.level=0.95)

    # age of high wearers is sig dif (adj p= 0.0275)

demo_weartime %>% group_by(wrist_cathours) %>% summarise(mean(age))


# PLOTS

# ankle band box
ggplot(demo_weartime, aes(x=ankle_cathours, y=age, fill=ankle_cathours)) + geom_boxplot() + labs(title= "Boxplot of Ankle Cat by Age") + theme(legend.position= "none")

# wrist band box
ggplot(demo_weartime, aes(x=wrist_cathours, y=age, fill=wrist_cathours)) + geom_boxplot() + labs(title= "Boxplot of Wrist Cat by Age") + theme(legend.position= "none")
```

## c. long sleeves/ pants
```{r}

# selecting variables with sleeve/ pant data
sleeves_pants_day <- bands %>% 
  select(S_ID, 
         contains("sleeve"), 
         contains("pant"),
         contains("daytime"), 
         contains("week")) %>%  
  rename("child_wrist_day1" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime1_T30", 
         "child_ankle_day1" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime1_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_day1"= "bwb_pwhrsdaytime1_T30",
         "child_wrist_day2" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime2_T30",
         "child_ankle_day2" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime2_T30",
         "parent_wrist_day2" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime2_T30",
         "child_wrist_day3" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime3_T30", 
         "child_ankle_day3" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime3_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_day3" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime3_T30",
         "child_wrist_day4" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime4_T30", 
         "child_ankle_day4" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime4_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_day4" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime4_T30",
         "child_wrist_day5" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime5_T30", 
         "child_ankle_day5" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime5_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_day5" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime5_T30",
         "child_wrist_day6" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime6_T30", 
         "child_ankle_day6" = "bwb_cahrsdaytime6_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_day6" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime6_T30",
         "child_wrist_day7" = "bwb_cwhrsdaytime7_T30", 
         "child_ankle_day7" ="bwb_cahrsdaytime7_T30", 
         "parent_wrist_day7" = "bwb_pwhrsdaytime7_T30",
         
         "child_wrist_wktotal" = "bwb_cwhrsweek_T30",
         "child_wrist_daytotal" = "bwb_cwhrsweekday_T30",
         "child_wrist_nighttotal" = "bwb_cwhrsweeknite_T30",
         "child_ankle_wktotal" = "bwb_cahrsweek_T30",
         "child_ankle_daytotal" = "bwb_cahrsweekday_T30",
         "child_ankle_nighttotal" = "bwb_cahrsweeknite_T30",
         
         "day1_sleeves" = "bwb_day1_cwsleeves_T30",
         "day2_sleeves" = "bwb_day2_cwsleeves_T30", 
         "day3_sleeves" = "bwb_day3_cwsleeves_T30",
         "day4_sleeves" = "bwb_day4_cwsleeves_T30", 
         "day5_sleeves" = "bwb_day5_cwsleeves_T30", 
         "day6_sleeves" = "bwb_day6_cwsleeves_T30", 
         "day7_sleeves" = "bwb_day7_cwsleeves_T30", 
         
         "day1_pants" = "bwb_day1_capants_T30",
         "day2_pants" = "bwb_day2_capants_T30",
         "day3_pants" = "bwb_day3_capants_T30", 
         "day4_pants" = "bwb_day4_capants_T30", 
         "day5_pants" = "bwb_day5_capants_T30", 
         "day6_pants" = "bwb_day6_capants_T30", 
         "day7_pants" = "bwb_day7_capants_T30")

sleeves_pants_day_long <- sleeves_pants_day %>% 
  select(S_ID, contains("sleeve"), contains("pants")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(!S_ID, names_to = "longname", values_to= "present")

sleeves_wrist <- sleeves_pants_day %>% 
  select(S_ID, (contains("wrist") & contains("child")), contains("sleeve")) %>% 
  mutate(n.na=rowSums(is.na(select(.,c(day1_sleeves:day7_sleeves)))), 
         n.yes=rowSums(select(.,c(day1_sleeves:day7_sleeves)) == 1, na.rm=TRUE),
         n.no=rowSums((select(.,c(day1_sleeves:day7_sleeves))) == 0, na.rm=TRUE)) %>% 
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(n.total=sum(c_across(c(n.na:n.no))),
         sleeves_per_yes= n.yes / 7) %>% #check
  ungroup()


# without removing rows with all missing
sleeves_percent_scatter_all <- ggplot(sleeves_wrist, aes(x=sleeves_per_yes, y=child_wrist_daytotal)) + 
  geom_point(color="orange") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.y=c(30,35), digits= 3) +
  labs(title= "Daytime Wristband Hours by Percent of the week wearing sleeves") + 
  ylab("Total Daytime hours") + 
  xlab("Percent of the week wearing sleeves")
  
sleeves_percent_scatter_all 

#removing the participants that had n.na=7 (all missing)
sleeves_wrist <- sleeves_wrist %>% 
  filter(!n.na==7)

sleeves_percent_scatter_noallna <- ggplot(sleeves_wrist, aes(x=sleeves_per_yes, y=child_wrist_daytotal)) + 
  geom_point(color="orange") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.y=c(30,35), digits= 3) +
  labs(title= "Daytime Wristband Hours by Percent of the week wearing sleeves") + 
  ylab("Total Daytime hours") + 
  xlab("Percent of the week wearing sleeves")
  
sleeves_percent_scatter_noallna 

pants_ankle <- sleeves_pants_day %>% 
  select(S_ID, contains("ankle"), contains("pant")) %>% 
  mutate(n.na=rowSums(is.na(select(.,c(day1_pants:day7_pants)))), 
         n.yes=rowSums(select(.,c(day1_pants:day7_pants)) == 1, na.rm=TRUE),
         n.no=rowSums((select(.,c(day1_pants:day7_pants))) == 0, na.rm=TRUE)) %>% 
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(n.total=sum(c_across(c(n.na:n.no))),
         pants_per_yes= n.yes / 7) %>% #check
  ungroup()

pants_percent_scatter_all <- ggplot(pants_ankle, aes(x=pants_per_yes, y=child_ankle_daytotal)) + 
  geom_point(color="cornflowerblue") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.y=c(30,35)) +
  labs(title= "Daytime Ankle Band Hours by Percent of the week wearing sleeves") + 
  ylab("Total Daytime hours") + 
  xlab("Percent of the week wearing pants")

pants_percent_scatter_all

# removing the participants that had n.na=7 (all missing)
pants_ankle <- pants_ankle %>% 
  filter(!n.na==7)

pants_percent_scatter_noallna <- ggplot(pants_ankle, aes(x=pants_per_yes, y=child_ankle_daytotal)) + 
  geom_point(color="cornflowerblue") + 
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.y=c(30,35), digits= 3) +
  labs(title= "Daytime Ankle Band Hours by Percent of the week wearing sleeves") + 
  ylab("Total Daytime hours") + 
  xlab("Percent of the week wearing pants")

pants_percent_scatter_noallna

```

## d. childcare
### ccare1_T30: Childcare Q1. Typical week, how many places does child receive care outside of self and live-in partner?
```{r}

# look at counts for how many places the child receives care outside the home in a typical week (range = 0-5)
bands %>% count(ccare1_T30)

# categorize to collapse small categories 
childcare <- bands %>% 
  select(S_ID, ccare1_T30) %>% 
  mutate(childcare_cat = case_when(ccare1_T30== 0 ~ "0",             
         # 3 categories
                                   ccare1_T30== 1 ~ "1", 
                                   ccare1_T30== 2 ~ "2 or more", 
                                   ccare1_T30== 3 ~ "2 or more", 
                                   ccare1_T30== 4 ~ "2 or more",
                                   ccare1_T30== 5 ~ "2 or more"),
         childcare_bi = case_when(ccare1_T30== 0 ~ "No chilcare",     
         # binary, childcare/no
                                   ccare1_T30== 1 ~ "Childcare", 
                                   ccare1_T30== 2 ~ "Childcare", 
                                   ccare1_T30== 3 ~ "Childcare", 
                                   ccare1_T30== 4 ~ "Childcare",
                                   ccare1_T30== 5 ~ "Childcare"), 
         childcare_cat_num = case_when(ccare1_T30== 0 ~ 0,            
         # 3 categories, as numeric for anova
                                   ccare1_T30== 1 ~ 1, 
                                   ccare1_T30== 2 ~ 2, 
                                   ccare1_T30== 3 ~ 2, 
                                   ccare1_T30== 4 ~ 2,
                                   ccare1_T30== 5 ~ 2))
# checks
childcare %>% count(childcare_cat)
childcare %>% count(childcare_cat_num)
childcare %>% count(childcare_bi)
# counts match the noncollapsed vars 


# merge with wear time
table3_childcare <- merge(childcare, demo_weartime)
# table3_childcare %>% group_by(wrist_cat6day) %>% count(childcare_cat)
# table3_childcare %>% group_by(ankle_cat6day) %>% count(childcare_cat)

hist(table3_childcare$wrist_hours) # non normal 




# WRIST

# kruskal wallis test for multiple comparisons (non normal data)
kruskal.test(wrist_hours ~ childcare_cat, data= table3_childcare)

# plot

# 3 group comparison
childcare_wrist_box <- ggplot(table3_childcare, aes(x=childcare_cat, y=wrist_hours, fill=childcare_cat)) + 
  geom_boxplot(alpha=1, fill= "orange", outlier.shape=NA) +
  stat_kruskal_test(label= "Kruskal-Wallis, p = {p.format}", label.y= 86) +
  geom_jitter(size=0.5, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) +
  labs(title= "Child Wrist Band Total Wear Times by Number of Child Care facilities")

childcare_wrist_box

# 2 group comparison
childcarebi_wrist_box <- ggplot(table3_childcare, aes(x=childcare_bi, y=wrist_hours, fill=childcare_bi)) + 
  geom_boxplot(alpha=1, fill= "orange", outlier.shape=NA) + 
  geom_jitter(size=0.5, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) + 
  labs(title= "Child Wrist Band Total Wear Times by Number of Child Care facilities") +
  stat_compare_means(method= "wilcox.test", label.y = 90)

childcarebi_wrist_box 




# ANKLE

hist(table3_childcare$ankle_hours) # non normal 

# kruskal wallis test for multiple comparisons (non normal data)
kruskal.test(ankle_hours ~ childcare_cat, data= table3_childcare)

# plot

# 3 group comparison
childcare_ankle_box <- ggplot(table3_childcare, aes(x=childcare_cat, y=ankle_hours, fill=childcare_cat)) + 
  geom_boxplot(alpha=1, fill= "cornflowerblue", outlier.shape=NA) + 
  stat_kruskal_test(label= "Kruskal-Wallis, p = {p.format}", label.y= 86) +
  geom_jitter(size=0.5, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) + 
  labs(title= "Child Ankle Band Total Wear Times by Number of Child Care facilities")

childcare_ankle_box

# 2 group comparison
childcarebi_ankle_box <- ggplot(table3_childcare, aes(x=childcare_bi, y=ankle_hours, fill=childcare_bi)) + 
  geom_boxplot(alpha=1, fill= "cornflowerblue", outlier.shape=NA) + 
  geom_jitter(size=0.5, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) + 
  labs(title= "Child Ankle Band Total Wear Times by Number of Child Care facilities") + 
  stat_compare_means(method= "wilcox.test", label.y = 90)

childcarebi_ankle_box



```

## e. disabilities
### cdc Does child have any suspected or identified disabilities?
```{r}

# select variable and merge with wear time 
## 
cdc <- bands %>% 
  select(S_ID, cdc_disany_T30) 

cdc <- merge(cdc, wk_child_day_hours)

cdc %>% count(cdc_disany_T30)
  # 0 == none       N=83
  # 1 == yes        N=19
  # NA == missing   N=13

cdc <- cdc %>% 
  filter(!is.na(cdc_disany_T30)) %>% 
  mutate(disany_binary=case_when(cdc_disany_T30 == 0 ~ "none", 
                                 cdc_disany_T30 == 1 ~ "one"))

# WRIST 
cdc_wrist_wilcox <- wilcox_test(data=cdc, wrist_hours ~ disany_binary)
formatted_stat <- format.pval(cdc_wrist_wilcox$statistic, digits = 3)
print(formatted_stat)

# ANKLE
cdc_ankle_wilcox <- wilcox_test(data=cdc, ankle_hours ~ disany_binary)
formatted_stat <- format.pval(cdc_ankle_wilcox$statistic, digits = 3)
print(formatted_stat)
```

## f. spearman correlations between attributes and child wrist and ankle wear time
```{r}

# WRIST
attributes <- as.vector(colnames(wk_child_day_wrist[5:15]))
is.vector(attributes)

# empty df to store results
results_df <- data.frame(
  variable = character(),
  correlation = numeric(),
  p_value = numeric(),
  conf_lower = numeric(),
  conf_upper = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# spearman correlation for each variable in the list
for (var in attributes) {
  test_result <- cor.test(wk_child_day_wrist[[var]], wk_child_day_wrist$hours, method= "spearman")
  
  p_value <- ifelse(is.na(test_result$p.value), NA, test_result$p.value)
  
  # append the results to the dataframe
  results_df <- rbind(results_df, data.frame(
    variable = var,
    correlation = test_result$estimate,
    p_value = p_value,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ))
}

# rename 
corr_child_atttributesdaywear_wrist <- results_df

# check for psi_mn
cor.test(wk_child_day_wrist$PSI_Mn_T30, wk_child_day_wrist$hours, method= "spearman")




# ANKLE
attributes <- as.vector(colnames(wk_child_day_ankle[5:15]))
is.vector(attributes)

# empty df to store results
results_df <- data.frame(
  variable = character(),
  correlation = numeric(),
  p_value = numeric(),
  conf_lower = numeric(),
  conf_upper = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# spearman correlation for each variable in the list
for (var in attributes) {
  test_result <- cor.test(wk_child_day_ankle[[var]], wk_child_day_ankle$hours, method= "spearman")
  
  p_value <- ifelse(is.na(test_result$p.value), NA, test_result$p.value)
  
  # append the results to the dataframe
  results_df <- rbind(results_df, data.frame(
    variable = var,
    correlation = test_result$estimate,
    p_value = p_value,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ))
}

# rename 
corr_child_atttributesdaywear_ankle <- results_df

# check
cor.test(wk_child_day_ankle$PSI_Mn_T30, wk_child_day_ankle$hours, method= "spearman")

```

## g. wilcox rank sum tests to see if wear time groups have dif attribute scores (low/mod; less than 72 hours vs. high ≥72 hours)
### table 3
```{r}

# modified from TAME2 Ch4 "Unpaired example" https://uncsrp.github.io/TAME2/two-group-comparisons-and-visualizations.html#unpaired-test-example

# WRIST 
endpoints <- colnames(wk_child_day_wrist %>% select(PSI_Mn_T30:PAS_AVG_T30)) 

# create dataframe to store results
cat_hours_wrist_wilcoxres <- data.frame()

# run for loop
for (i in 1:length(endpoints)) {
  
  # assign a name to the endpoint variable.
  endpoint <- endpoints[i]
  
  # run wilcox test and store in results dataframe.
  res_df <- wk_child_day_wrist %>%
    wilcox_test(as.formula(paste0(endpoint, "~ cat_hours", sep = "")))
  
  # bind results from this test with other tests in this loop
  cat_hours_wrist_wilcoxres <- rbind(cat_hours_wrist_wilcoxres, res_df)
  
}

cat_hours_wrist_wilcoxres

#ANKLE
endpoints <- colnames(wk_child_day_ankle %>% select(PSI_Mn_T30:PAS_AVG_T30)) 

# create dataframe to store results
cat_hours_ankle_wilcoxres <- data.frame()

# run for loop
for (i in 1:length(endpoints)) {
  
  # assign a name to the endpoint variable.
  endpoint <- endpoints[i]
  
  # run wilcox test and store in results dataframe.
  res_df <- wk_child_day_ankle %>%
    wilcox_test(as.formula(paste0(endpoint, "~ cat_hours", sep = ""))) 
  
  # bind results from this test with other tests in this loop
  cat_hours_ankle_wilcoxres <- rbind(cat_hours_ankle_wilcoxres, res_df)
  
}

# manual look at the means 
wk_child_day_ankle %>% 
  filter(cat_hours=="< 72 hours") %>% 
  summarise(mean(ECBQ_EFFCO_T30, na.rm=TRUE))

wk_child_day_ankle %>% 
  filter(cat_hours=="72 or more hours") %>% 
  summarise(mean(ECBQ_EFFCO_T30, na.rm=TRUE))

```

## h. additional child characteristics (sex, childcare) and parent attributes (income to needs, education, age, stress)
### table 3
```{r}

# df of demo and additional attributes  
additional_attributes <- bands %>% 
  select(S_ID, 
         TC_AgeMonths_Home_T30, 
         MCDI3_All_T30, #all 
         MCDI3_Use_T30, #language only
         PAS_AVG_T30,   #total anxiety
         BIQ_Soc_T30,   
         BIQ_Nov_T30, 
         BIQ_Tot_T30,   #total 
         ECBQ_dis_T30, 
         ECBQ_NEGAF_T30, 
         ECBQ_SURGE_T30, 
         ECBQ_EFFCO_T30,
         income2needs_T30, 
         PC_matage_home_T30,
         pc_ed,
         PSI_Mn_T30,   #mean across all items
         PSI_DC_T30)

additional_attributes <- merge(demo_weartime, additional_attributes, by="S_ID")

additional_attributes <- additional_attributes %>% 
  select(-latino_hispanic, 
         -race) %>% 
  relocate(adult_hours, .after=PSI_DC_T30)

# WILCOX RANK SUM for WRIST
endpoints <- colnames(additional_attributes %>% select(TC_AgeMonths_Home_T30:adult_hours)) 

# create dataframe to store results
addatt_wrist_wilcoxres <- data.frame()

# run for loop
for (i in 1:length(endpoints)) {
  
  # assign a name to the endpoint variable.
  endpoint <- endpoints[i]
  
  # calculate means for the two groups
  means <- additional_attributes %>%
    group_by(wrist_cat6day) %>%
    summarise(mean_value = mean(get(endpoint), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = wrist_cat6day, values_from = mean_value, names_prefix = "mean_")
  
  # run Wilcoxon test and store in results dataframe.
  res_df <- additional_attributes %>%
    wilcox_test(as.formula(paste0(endpoint, "~ wrist_cat6day", sep = ""))) %>%
    left_join(means, by = character()) %>% 
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3)))
  
  # bind results from this test with other tests in this loop
  addatt_wrist_wilcoxres <- rbind(addatt_wrist_wilcoxres, res_df)
  
}

addatt_wrist_wilcoxres

is.numeric(addatt_wrist_wilcoxres$statistic)

# WILCOX RANK SUM for ANKLE

# create dataframe to store results
addatt_ankle_wilcoxres <- data.frame()

# run for loop
for (i in 1:length(endpoints)) {
  
  # assign a name to the endpoint variable.
  endpoint <- endpoints[i]
  
  # calculate means for the two groups
  means <- additional_attributes %>%
    group_by(ankle_cat6day) %>%
    summarise(mean_value = mean(get(endpoint), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = ankle_cat6day, values_from = mean_value, names_prefix = "Mean_")
  
  # run Wilcoxon test and store in results dataframe.
  res_df <- additional_attributes %>%
    wilcox_test(as.formula(paste0(endpoint, "~ ankle_cat6day", sep = ""))) %>%
    mutate(Variable = endpoint) %>%
    left_join(means, by = character())
  
  # bind results from this test with other tests in this loop
  addatt_ankle_wilcoxres <- rbind(addatt_ankle_wilcoxres, res_df)
  
}

addatt_ankle_wilcoxres

addatt_ankle_wilcoxres$statistic <- round(addatt_ankle_wilcoxres$statistic, digits = 3)

# WRIST
# check with the counts
additional_attributes %>% group_by(wrist_cat6day) %>% count(wrist_cat6day)
# mean age of the two groups
additional_attributes %>% group_by(wrist_cat6day) %>% summarise(mean= mean(age))
# sex distribution within the two groups
additional_attributes %>% group_by(wrist_cat6day) %>% count(sex)

# ANKLE
# check 
additional_attributes %>% group_by(ankle_cat6day) %>% count(ankle_cat6day)
# mean age of the two groups
additional_attributes %>% group_by(ankle_cat6day) %>% summarise(mean= mean(age))
# sex distribution within the two groups
additional_attributes %>% group_by(ankle_cat6day) %>% count(sex)



## SEX IS CATEGORICAL VAR ##

# assessing independence in the weartime groups by sex
chisq_ankle_sex <- chisq.test(additional_attributes$sex, additional_attributes$ankle_cat_hours)
format(round(chisq_ankle_sex$p.value, 3), nsmall = 3)

chisq_wrist_sex <-chisq.test(additional_attributes$sex, additional_attributes$wrist_cat_hours)
format(round(chisq_wrist_sex$p.value, 3), nsmall = 3)


# creating new dataframe to do wilcox test comparing mean use of long sleeves/pants over the 7 day week
sleeves_addatt <- sleeves_wrist %>% 
  select(S_ID, sleeves_per_yes)

sleeves_addatt <- merge(sleeves_addatt, additional_attributes, by="S_ID")

sleeves_wilcox <- wilcox.test(data=sleeves_addatt, sleeves_per_yes ~ wrist_cat6day)
format(round(sleeves_wilcox$p.value, 3), nsmall = 3)


pants_addatt <- pants_ankle %>% 
  select(S_ID, pants_per_yes)

pants_addatt <- merge(pants_addatt, additional_attributes, by="S_ID")

pants_wilcox <- wilcox.test(data=pants_addatt, pants_per_yes ~ ankle_cat6day)
format(round(pants_wilcox$p.value, 3), nsmall = 3)

### CORRELATIONS ###

# parental stress and parent wear time (only wrist)
# mean of PSI
cor.test(wk_adult_day$PSI_Mn_T30, wk_adult_day$hours, method= "spearman")
# difficult child
cor.test(wk_adult_day$PSI_DC_T30, wk_adult_day$hours, method= "spearman")

## child wrist and ankle wear time correlations with PSI are in respective dfs (corr_child_atttributesdaywear_ankle/ corr_child_atttributesdaywear_wrist)



### INCOME TO NEEDS
# wrist 
cor.test(additional_attributes$income2needs_T30, additional_attributes$wrist_hours, method="spearman")

# ankle
cor.test(additional_attributes$income2needs_T30, additional_attributes$ankle_hours, method="spearman")



### mat edu
# wrist 
cor.test(additional_attributes$pc_ed, additional_attributes$wrist_hours, method="spearman")

# ankle 
cor.test(additional_attributes$pc_ed, additional_attributes$ankle_hours, method="spearman")



### MAT AGE
# wrist 
cor.test(additional_attributes$PC_matage_home_T30, additional_attributes$wrist_hours, method="spearman")

# ankle
cor.test(additional_attributes$PC_matage_home_T30, additional_attributes$ankle_hours, method="spearman")

```

## i. linear regression
### 1. unadjusted (only attribute) 
### 2. attribute and adjusted for age and sex
```{r}

regressiondf <- bands %>%  # attributes we found to be significantly different at p<0.05 between the low/mod and high wear time groups
  select(S_ID, 
         MCDI3_All_T30,    # wrist
         PAS_AVG_T30,      # wrist
         BIQ_Tot_T30,      # ankle
         BIQ_Nov_T30,      # ankle
         BIQ_Soc_T30,      # ankle
         ECBQ_EFFCO_T30)   # ankle

demo_weartime_reg <- demo_weartime %>% 
  select(S_ID, sex, age, wrist_hours, ankle_hours)

regressiondf <- merge(regressiondf, demo_weartime_reg, by="S_ID")

## UNADJUSTED
# WRIST

## MCDI
regression_wrist_mcdi <- lm(wrist_hours ~ MCDI3_All_T30,
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_wrist_mcdi_crudesumm <- summary(regression_wrist_mcdi)
regression_wrist_mcdi_conf <- confint(regression_wrist_mcdi, level= 0.95)

regression_wrist_mcdi_crudesumm
regression_wrist_mcdi_conf

## PAS
regression_wrist_pas <- lm(wrist_hours ~ PAS_AVG_T30, 
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_wrist_pas_crudesumm <- summary(regression_wrist_pas)
regression_wrist_pas_conf <- confint(regression_wrist_pas, level= 0.95)

regression_wrist_pas_crudesumm
regression_wrist_pas_conf



# ANKLE 

## ECBQ Effortful control
regression_ankle_ecbq <- lm(ankle_hours ~ ECBQ_EFFCO_T30, 
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_ankle_ecbq_crudesumm <- summary(regression_ankle_ecbq)
regression_ankle_ecbq_conf <- confint(regression_ankle_ecbq, level= 0.95)

regression_ankle_ecbq_crudesumm
regression_ankle_ecbq_conf

## BIQ social 
regression_ankle_biqsoc <- lm(ankle_hours ~ BIQ_Soc_T30, 
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_ankle_biqsoc_crudesumm <- summary(regression_ankle_biqsoc)
regression_ankle_biqsoc_conf <- confint(regression_ankle_biqsoc, level= 0.95)

regression_ankle_biqsoc_crudesumm
regression_ankle_biqsoc_conf

## BIQ novelty
regression_ankle_biqnov <- lm(ankle_hours ~ BIQ_Nov_T30, 
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_ankle_biqnov_crudesumm <- summary(regression_ankle_biqnov)
regression_ankle_biqnov_conf <- confint(regression_ankle_biqnov, level= 0.95)

regression_ankle_biqnov_crudesumm
regression_ankle_biqnov_conf

## BIQ total
regression_ankle_biqtot <- lm(ankle_hours ~ BIQ_Tot_T30, 
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_ankle_biqtot_cudesumm <- summary(regression_ankle_biqtot)
regression_ankle_biqtot_conf <- confint(regression_ankle_biqtot, level= 0.95)

regression_ankle_biqtot_cudesumm
regression_ankle_biqtot_conf






## ADJUSTED
# WRIST

## MCDI
regression_wrist_mcdi <- lm(wrist_hours ~ MCDI3_All_T30 + age + sex,
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_wrist_mcdi_adjsumm <- summary(regression_wrist_mcdi)
regression_wrist_mcdi_adjconf <- confint(regression_wrist_mcdi, level= 0.95)

regression_wrist_mcdi_adjsumm
regression_wrist_mcdi_adjconf

## PAS
regression_wrist_pas <- lm(wrist_hours ~ PAS_AVG_T30 + age + sex, 
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_wrist_pas_adjsumm <- summary(regression_wrist_pas)
regression_wrist_pas_adjconf <- confint(regression_wrist_pas, level= 0.95)

regression_wrist_pas_adjsumm
regression_wrist_pas_adjconf



# ANKLE 

## ECBQ Effortful control
regression_ankle_ecbq <- lm(ankle_hours ~ ECBQ_EFFCO_T30 + age + sex, 
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_ankle_ecbq_adjsumm <- summary(regression_ankle_ecbq)
regression_ankle_ecbq_adjconf <- confint(regression_ankle_ecbq, level= 0.95)

regression_ankle_ecbq_adjsumm
regression_ankle_ecbq_adjconf

## BIQ social 
regression_ankle_biqsoc <- lm(ankle_hours ~ BIQ_Soc_T30 + age + sex, 
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_ankle_biqsoc_adjsumm <- summary(regression_ankle_biqsoc)
regression_ankle_biqsoc_adjconf <- confint(regression_ankle_biqsoc, level= 0.95)

regression_ankle_biqsoc_adjsumm
regression_ankle_biqsoc_adjconf

## BIQ novelty
regression_ankle_biqnov <- lm(ankle_hours ~ BIQ_Nov_T30 + age + sex, 
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_ankle_biqnov_adjsumm <- summary(regression_ankle_biqnov)
regression_ankle_biqnov_adjconf <- confint(regression_ankle_biqnov, level= 0.95)

regression_ankle_biqnov_adjsumm
regression_ankle_biqnov_adjconf

## BIQ total
regression_ankle_biqtot <- lm(ankle_hours ~ BIQ_Tot_T30 + age + sex, 
                       data=regressiondf)
regression_ankle_biqtot_adjsumm <- summary(regression_ankle_biqtot)
regression_ankle_biqtot_adjconf <- confint(regression_ankle_biqtot, level= 0.95)

regression_ankle_biqtot_adjsumm
regression_ankle_biqtot_adjconf

```

# RQ3: WHAT RECOMMENDATIONS CAN WE PROVIDE TO IMPROVE COMPLIANCE AND PARTICIPANT EXPERIENCES FOR FUTURE STUDIES?

## a. issues
```{r}

# issues with the bands from the qualitative, write-in question for the band diary
issues_nobwb <- issues %>% 
  select(-contains("bwb"))

demo_issues_weartime <- merge(demo_weartime, issues_nobwb, by="S_ID", all.x= TRUE) 

demo_issues_weartime_long <- demo_issues_weartime %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Size)) %>% # those with missing for size, missing for all
  select(-ankle_cat6day, -wrist_cat6day, 
         -adult_hours, -latino_hispanic, -race, -ankle_cat_hours, -wrist_cat_hours) %>% #relevant variables
  pivot_longer(!c(S_ID, ankle_hours, wrist_hours, combined_total, ankle_cathours, wrist_cathours, age, sex), names_to = "issue", values_to = "present") 

sum_issues <- demo_issues_weartime_long %>% 
  group_by(issue) %>% 
  summarise(sum=sum(present))

issues_long <- issues_nobwb %>% 
  pivot_longer(!S_ID, names_to = "type", values_to = "present") %>% 
  filter(present==1) #to include only those that had an issue (no 0s)


sum_issues_bar <- issues_long %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=type)) +
  geom_bar(stat= "count", fill= "darkgreen") 

sum_issues_bar


demo_issues_weartime <- demo_issues_weartime %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Size)) %>% 
  select(-ankle_cat_hours, -wrist_cat_hours, -wrist_cathours, -ankle_cathours)

# are the same people leaving comments?
demo_issues_weartime$composite_comments <- rowSums(demo_issues_weartime[, c(12:18, 20:21)], na.rm=TRUE) #column 19 is no notes
demo_issues_weartime$composite_nopos <- rowSums(demo_issues_weartime[, c(12:15,17, 18, 20, 21)], na.rm=TRUE)
                                                                            #removes column 16 (positive) and 19 (no notes)

demo_issues_weartime %>% count(composite_comments) #including the positive comments

demo_issues_weartime %>% count(composite_nopos) #not including the positive comments, only issues

# sample size for 3 and 4 comments is a bit small, going to look at wear time based on 0, 1, and 2 or more comments
demo_issues_weartime <- demo_issues_weartime %>% 
  mutate(comment_cat = case_when(composite_nopos == 0 ~ "0", 
                                composite_nopos == 1 ~ "1",
```
