```{r}
                                composite_nopos == 2 ~ "2 or more", 
                                  composite_nopos == 3 ~ "2 or more",
                                  composite_nopos == 4  ~ "2 or more"
                                ))
demo_issues_weartime %>% 
  count(comment_cat)


# issues to wrist hours
issues_wrist_box <- ggplot(demo_issues_weartime, aes(x=comment_cat, y=wrist_hours, fill=comment_cat)) +
  geom_boxplot( outlier.shape = NA, fill= "orange") +
  labs(title= "Daytime Wristband Wear by Number of Issues") +
  ylab("Day Time Wrist Hours") +
  xlab("Number of Issues") +
  geom_jitter(size=0.3, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) + 
  stat_kruskal_test(label= "Kruskal-Wallis, p = {p.format}", label.y= 86) 
 
issues_wrist_box

# kruskal wallis test for multiple comparisons
kruskal.test(wrist_hours ~ comment_cat, data= demo_issues_weartime)

# post hoc test for multiple comparisons (dunn's test)
dunnTest(wrist_hours ~ comment_cat, data=demo_issues_weartime, 
         method="bonferroni")


issues_ankle_box <- ggplot(demo_issues_weartime, aes(x=comment_cat, y=ankle_hours, fill=comment_cat)) +
  geom_boxplot( outlier.shape = NA, fill= "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title= "Daytime Ankleband Wear by Number of Issues") +
  ylab("Day Time Ankle Hours") +
  xlab("Number of Issues") +
  geom_jitter(size=0.3, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) + 
  stat_kruskal_test(label= "Kruskal-Wallis, p = {p.format}", label.y= 86) 
 
issues_ankle_box

kruskal.test(ankle_hours ~ comment_cat, data= demo_issues_weartime)

# post hoc test for multiple comparisons (dunn's test)
dunnTest(ankle_hours ~ comment_cat, data=demo_issues_weartime, 
         method="bonferroni")






ankle_issues_weartime <- demo_issues_weartime %>% 
  select(-wrist_hours) %>% #keep everything but the wrist wear time
  rename("hours"= "ankle_hours") %>% 
  mutate(position= "ankle")

wrist_issues_weartime <- demo_issues_weartime %>% 
  select(-ankle_hours) %>%
  rename("hours"= "wrist_hours") %>% 
  mutate(position= "wrist")

position_issues_weartime <- rbind(ankle_issues_weartime, wrist_issues_weartime)

## paired
issues_byposition <- ggplot(position_issues_weartime, aes(x=comment_cat, y=hours, fill=position)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha= 1, outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("wrist" = "orange", 
                               "ankle" = "cornflowerblue")) +
  labs(title= "Daytime Band Wear by Number of Issues") +
  ylab("Day Time Hours") +
  xlab("Number of Comments") +
  geom_jitter(size=0.3, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test", paired = TRUE,
                      label.y = 90, size = 5)

issues_byposition


issues_wrist_bysex <- ggplot(demo_issues_weartime, aes(x=comment_cat, y=wrist_hours, fill=sex)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha= 1, outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("F" = "darkorange", 
                               "M" = "aquamarine3")) +
  labs(title= "Daytime Wristband Wear by Number of Issues (by sex)") +
  ylab("Day Time Wrist Hours") +
  xlab("Number of Comments") +
  geom_jitter(size=0.3, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                      label.y = 90, size = 5)
  
issues_wrist_bysex


issues_ankle_bysex <- ggplot(demo_issues_weartime, aes(x=comment_cat, y=ankle_hours, fill=sex)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha= 1, outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("F" = "darkorange", 
                               "M" = "aquamarine3")) +
  labs(title= "Daytime Ankle Band Wear by Number of Issues (by sex)") +
  ylab("Day Time Ankle Hours") +
  xlab("Number of Comments") +
  geom_jitter(size=0.3, alpha= 0.9, position = position_jitterdodge(0.07)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                      label.y = 90, size = 5)
  
issues_ankle_bysex

```


Random forest code by allison spring 
```{r}

set.seed(12)

# random forest
randomforest_demo_data <- data %>%
  filter(!S_ID == 1114, 
         !S_ID == 1219, 
         !S_ID == 1023, 
         !S_ID == 2003, 
         !S_ID == 1015, 
         !S_ID == 1097) %>%  
  #select(S_ID:tc_race_other) %>%
  select(S_ID, TC_AgeMonths_Home_T30,PC_matage_home_T30,TC_Female,income2needs_T30,pc_ed) %>%
  #select(-c(box_wrist, inventorynotes_wrist, returned_wrist, returned_ankle, box_ankle, inventorynotes_ankle, Complete_Home_T30, GADays_Home_T30)) %>%
  rename("child_age_months_t30" = "TC_AgeMonths_Home_T30", 
         "mat_age_t30" = "PC_matage_home_T30","child_sex"="TC_Female","Maternal_Education"="pc_ed")



#Merge the parent and child characteristics with total number of hours worn
randomforest_data <- merge(attributes_weartimetotals, randomforest_demo_data, by="S_ID")%>%
  select(-c("parent_parentwrist_nighttotal","child_wrist_wktotal","child_ankle_wktotal","child_ankle_nighttotal","parent_parentwrist_wktotal", "S_ID")) %>%
  rename("PSI_Total_Parenting_Stress"="PSI_Mn_T30")%>%
  rename("PSI_Parenting_Stress_for_a_Difficult_Child"="PSI_DC_T30")%>%
  rename("MCDI_Overall_Language"="MCDI3_All_T30")%>%
  rename("ECBQ_Discomfort"="ECBQ_dis_T30")%>%
  rename("ECBQ_Effortful_Control"="ECBQ_EFFCO_T30")%>%
  rename("ECBQ_Negative_Affect"="ECBQ_NEGAF_T30")%>%
  rename("BIQ_Inhibition_to_Situational_Novelty"="BIQ_Nov_T30")%>%
  rename("BIQ_Total_Behavioral_Inhibition"="BIQ_Tot_T30")%>%
  rename("BIQ_Inhibition_to_Social_Situations"="BIQ_Soc_T30")%>%
  rename("PAS_Total_Anxiety"="PAS_AVG_T30")%>%
  rename("Parent_Wristband"="parent_parentwrist_daytotal")%>%
  rename("Child_Sex"="child_sex")%>%
  rename("Family_Income_to_Needs_Ratio"="income2needs_T30")%>%
  rename("Maternal_Education"="Maternal_Education")%>%
  rename("Maternal_Age"="mat_age_t30")%>%
  rename("Child_Age"="child_age_months_t30")%>%
  rename("ECBQ_Surgency"="ECBQ_SURGE_T30")





#Complete Cases
randomforest_data_complete_cases <- randomforest_data[complete.cases(randomforest_data), ]


ankle<-randomforest_data_complete_cases[,c(-12)]#Remove S_ID and wrist
wrist<-randomforest_data_complete_cases[,c(-13)] #Remove S_ID and ankle

# 5-fold cross validation
index = createFolds(wrist$child_wrist_daytotal, k = 5) 

# Seeing if about 20% of the records are in the testing set
kfold1 = index[[1]]
length(kfold1)/nrow(wrist)

# Creating vectors for parameters to be tuned
ntree_values = c(50, 250, 500) # number of decision trees 
p = dim(wrist)[2] - 1 # number of predictor variables in the dataset
mtry_values = c(sqrt(p), p/2, p) # number of predictors to be used in the model


# Creating an empty dataframe to save the confusion matrix metrics and variable importance
metrics = data.frame()
variable_importance_df = data.frame()

# Iterating through the cross validation folds
for (i in 1:length(index)){
  # Training data
  data_train = wrist[-index[[i]],]
  
  # Test data
  data_test = wrist[index[[i]],]
  
  
  # Creating empty lists and dataframes to store errors 
  reg_rf_pred_tune = list()
  rf <-randomForest(child_wrist_daytotal~.,data=wrist, ntree=500) 
  mtry <- tuneRF(wrist[-13],wrist$child_wrist_daytotal, ntreeTry=500,
                 stepFactor=1.5,improve=0.01, trace=TRUE, plot=TRUE)
  best.m <- mtry[mtry[, 2] == min(mtry[, 2]), 1]
  
  
  # Now running RF on the entire training set with the tuned parameters
  # This will be done 5 times for each fold
  reg_rf <- randomForest(child_wrist_daytotal ~ ., data = data_train,
                         ntree = 500,
                         mtry = best.m)
  
  # Predicting on test set and adding the predicted values as an additional column to the test data
  data_test$wear_cat=case_when(data_test$child_wrist_daytotal<72~"L",
                               TRUE~"H")
  data_test$wear_cat=as.factor(data_test$wear_cat)
  data_test$Pred = predict(reg_rf, newdata = data_test, type = "response")
  data_test$Pred_cat=case_when(data_test$Pred<72~"L",
                               TRUE~"H")
  data_test$Pred_cat=as.factor(data_test$Pred_cat)
  levels(data_test$wear_cat) <- c("L", "H")
  levels(data_test$Pred_cat) <- c("L", "H")
  matrix = confusionMatrix(data = data_test$Pred_cat, 
                           reference = data_test$wear_cat)
  # Extracting accuracy, sens, spec, PPV, NPV and adding to the dataframe to take mean later
  matrix_values = data.frame(t(c(matrix$byClass[11])), t(c(matrix$byClass[1:4])))
  metrics = rbind(metrics, matrix_values)
  
  # Extracting variable importance
  variable_importance_values = data.frame(importance(reg_rf)) %>%
    rownames_to_column(var = "Predictor")
  variable_importance_df = rbind(variable_importance_df, variable_importance_values)
}

# Taking average across the 5 folds
metrics = metrics %>%
  summarise(`Balanced Accuracy` = mean(Balanced.Accuracy), Sensitivity = mean(Sensitivity), 
            Specificity = mean(Specificity), PPV = mean(Pos.Pred.Value), NPV = mean(Neg.Pred.Value,rm.na=TRUE))

variable_importance_df = variable_importance_df %>%
  group_by(Predictor) %>%
  summarise(MeanDecreaseGini = mean(IncNodePurity)) %>%
  # Sorting from highest to lowest
  arrange(-MeanDecreaseGini)

variable_importance_df
# Adding spaces between the variables that need the space
modified_variable_importance_df = variable_importance_df %>%
  mutate(Predictor = gsub("_", " ", Predictor))

# Saving the order of the variables from lowest to highest mean decrease gini by putting into a factor
predictor_order = rev(modified_variable_importance_df$Predictor)
modified_variable_importance_df$Predictor = factor(modified_variable_importance_df$Predictor, 
                                                   levels = predictor_order)

head(modified_variable_importance_df)
ggplot(data = modified_variable_importance_df , 
       aes(x = MeanDecreaseGini, y = Predictor, size = 2)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  theme_light() + 
  theme(axis.line = element_line(color = "black"), #making x and y axes black
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12), #changing size of x axis labels
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1.7)), #changes axis titles
        legend.title = element_text(face = 'bold', size = 14), #changes legend title
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12), #changes legend text
        strip.text.x = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"), #changes size of facet x axis 
        strip.text.y = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold")) + #changes size of facet y axis 
  labs(x = 'Importance on Wrist Wear Time', y = ' Predictor Variable') + #changing axis labels 
  guides(size = "none")#removing size legend


################################################################################
index = createFolds(ankle$child_ankle_daytotal, k = 5) 

# Seeing if about 20% of the records are in the testing set
kfold1 = index[[1]]
length(kfold1)/nrow(ankle)

# Creating vectors for parameters to be tuned
ntree_values = c(50, 250, 500) # number of decision trees 
p = dim(ankle)[2] - 1 # number of predictor variables in the dataset
mtry_values = c(sqrt(p), p/2, p) # number of predictors to be used in the model


# Creating an empty dataframe to save the confusion matrix metrics and variable importance
metrics = data.frame()
variable_importance_df = data.frame()

# Iterating through the cross validation folds
for (i in 1:length(index)){
  # Training data
  data_train = ankle[-index[[i]],]
  
  # Test data
  data_test = ankle[index[[i]],]
  
  #data_test$child_ankle_wktotal <- factor(data_test$child_ankle_wktotal)
  
  
  # Creating empty lists and dataframes to store errors 
  reg_rf_pred_tune = list()
  rf <-randomForest(child_ankle_daytotal~.,data=ankle, ntree=500) 
  mtry <- tuneRF(ankle[-13],ankle$child_ankle_daytotal, ntreeTry=500,
                 stepFactor=1.5,improve=0.01, trace=TRUE, plot=TRUE)
  best.m <- mtry[mtry[, 2] == min(mtry[, 2]), 1]
  
  # Tuning parameters: using ntree and mtry values to determine which combination yields the smallest OOB error 
  # from the validation datasets
  
  # Now running RF on the entire training set with the tuned parameters
  # This will be done 5 times for each fold
  reg_rf <- randomForest(child_ankle_daytotal ~ ., data = data_train,
                         ntree = 500,
                         mtry = best.m)
  
  # Predicting on test set and adding the predicted values as an additional column to the test data
  data_test$wear_cat=case_when(data_test$child_ankle_daytotal<72~"L",
                               TRUE~"H")
  data_test$wear_cat=as.factor(data_test$wear_cat)
  data_test$Pred = predict(reg_rf, newdata = data_test, type = "response")
  data_test$Pred_cat=case_when(data_test$Pred<72~"L",
                               TRUE~"H")
  data_test$Pred_cat=as.factor(data_test$Pred_cat)
  levels(data_test$wear_cat) <- c("L", "H")
  levels(data_test$Pred_cat) <- c("L", "H")
  matrix = confusionMatrix(data = data_test$Pred_cat, 
                           reference = data_test$wear_cat)
  # Extracting accuracy, sens, spec, PPV, NPV and adding to the dataframe to take mean later
  matrix_values = data.frame(t(c(matrix$byClass[11])), t(c(matrix$byClass[1:4])))
  metrics = rbind(metrics, matrix_values)
  
  # Extracting variable importance
  variable_importance_values = data.frame(importance(reg_rf)) %>%
    rownames_to_column(var = "Predictor")
  variable_importance_df = rbind(variable_importance_df, variable_importance_values)
}

# Taking average across the 5 folds
metrics = metrics %>%
  summarise(`Balanced Accuracy` = mean(Balanced.Accuracy), Sensitivity = mean(Sensitivity), 
            Specificity = mean(Specificity), PPV = mean(Pos.Pred.Value), NPV = mean(Neg.Pred.Value))

#Variable importance is a reflection of how much the performance of the model decreases when the variable is removed
variable_importance_df = variable_importance_df %>%
  group_by(Predictor) %>%
  summarise(MeanDecreaseGini = mean(IncNodePurity)) %>%
  #summarise(MeanDecreaseGini_std = stdev(IncNodePurity)) %>%
  # Sorting from highest to lowest
  arrange(-MeanDecreaseGini)

variable_importance_df
# Adding spaces between the variables that need the space
modified_variable_importance_df = variable_importance_df %>%
  mutate(Predictor = gsub("_", " ", Predictor))

# Saving the order of the variables from lowest to highest mean decrease gini by putting into a factor
predictor_order = rev(modified_variable_importance_df$Predictor)
modified_variable_importance_df$Predictor = factor(modified_variable_importance_df$Predictor, 
                                                   levels = predictor_order)

head(modified_variable_importance_df)

ggplot(data = modified_variable_importance_df , 
       aes(x = MeanDecreaseGini, y = Predictor, size = 2)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  
  theme_light() + 
  theme(axis.line = element_line(color = "black"), #making x and y axes black
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12), #changing size of x axis labels
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1.7)), #changes axis titles
        legend.title = element_text(face = 'bold', size = 14), #changes legend title
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12), #changes legend text
        strip.text.x = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"), #changes size of facet x axis 
        strip.text.y = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold")) + #changes size of facet y axis 
  labs(x = 'Importance on Ankle Wear Time', y = ' Predictor Variable') + #changing axis labels 
  
  guides(size = "none")#removing size legend


```

























maunscript plots

FIGURE 1-- box plot of child wrist and ankle band wear time by day and night time 
```{r}

figure1 <- ggplot(wksumsep, aes(x=day, y=hours, fill=position)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, lwd=1, alpha=1) +
  geom_jitter(size=1.5, position = position_jitterdodge(0.2)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                     paired= TRUE,
                     # Changing the value to asterisks and moving to the middle of the plot
                     label = "p.signif", label.x.npc = "center", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Child Ankle" = "lavender", 
                               "Child Wrist" = "#9999CC")) +
  # Adding padding y axis 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.3, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Total Hours") +
  xlab("Time of Day")
  
figure1

jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/raw/figure1.jpeg",
    width = 7, height = 5, units="in", res=1200)
figure1
invisible(dev.off())

```
FIGURE 2 was generated in powerpoint and illustrator

FIGURE 3 
boxplots with time of day (x) by total wear hours (y) 
including paired wilcox comparing wrist (dark) by ankle (light)
```{r}
# change the name of the 
demo_weartime_sep <- demo_weartime_sep %>% 
  mutate(position2=case_when(position == "ankle" ~ "Child Ankle", 
                            position == "wrist" ~ "Child Wrist"))


# paired, stratified by sex
figure3 <- ggplot(demo_weartime_sep, aes(x=sex, y=hours, fill= position2)) + 
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, lwd=1, alpha=1) +
  geom_jitter(size=1.5, position = position_jitterdodge(0.2)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                     paired= TRUE,
                     # Changing the value to asterisks and moving to the middle of the plot
                     label = "p.signif", label.x.npc = "center", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Child Ankle" = "lavender", 
                               "Child Wrist" = "#9999CC")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Female", "Male")) +
  # Adding padding y axis 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.3, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Total Daytime Hours") +
  xlab("Sex")
  
figure3



jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/raw/figure3.jpeg",
    width = 7, height = 5, units="in", res=1200)
figure3
invisible(dev.off())

```

FIGURE 4
select attributes for wristband
boxplots with categorized hours (x) by attribute scale (y) and wilcox test
scatter plots with total daytime hours (x) and attribute scale (y) and spearman correlation
attributes included: PAS and MCDI
```{r}

#separate wrist plots to be combined with patchwork
long_wk_child_day_wrist_pas <- long_wk_child_day %>% 
  filter(attribute== "PAS_AVG_T30", 
         position== "wrist")

#boxplot comparing categorized weartime (wilcox rank sum test)
figure4_pas <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_wrist_pas, aes(x=cat_hours, y=value, fill=cat_hours)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, lwd=1, alpha=1, position= position_dodge(width=5)) +
  geom_jitter(size=1.5, position = position_jitterdodge(0.3)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                     # Changing the value to asterisks and moving to the middle of the plot
                     label = "p.signif", label.x.npc = "center", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("< 72 hours" = "#f8c198", 
                               "72 or more hours" = "#e26f1a")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("<72 Hours", "≥72 Hours")) +
  # Adding padding y axis 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.1, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Categorized Hours") +
  labs(title= "PAS: Average\nTwo-Group Comparison",
       subtitle= "(p = 0.038)") 

figure4_pas

#jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/raw/figure4_pas.jpeg",
#    width = 160, height = 300, res=1000)
#figure4_pas
#invisible(dev.off())




#scatter with spearman correlation
figure4_pas_scat <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_wrist_pas, aes(x=hours, y=value)) + 
  geom_point(size= 1.5) + 
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=F, size=1.5, color="#e26f1a", linetype="dashed") +
  scale_shape_manual(values= c(16)) +
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(0.7,0.75), size=10, face="bold") + 
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.1, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Total Daytime Hours") + 
  labs(title= "PAS: Average\nCorrelation",
    subtitle= "(rho = 0.19, p = 0.085)")

figure4_pas_scat


#jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/raw/figure4_pas_scat.jpeg",
#    width = 2000, height = 2000)
#figure4_pas_scat
#invisible(dev.off())



#mcdi
long_wk_child_day_wrist_mcdi <- long_wk_child_day %>% 
  filter(attribute== "MCDI3_All_T30", 
         position== "wrist")


figure4_mcdi <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_wrist_mcdi, aes(x=cat_hours, y=value, fill=cat_hours)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, lwd=1, alpha=1, position= position_dodge(width=5)) +
  geom_jitter(size=1.5, position = position_jitterdodge(0.3)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                     # Changing the value to asterisks and moving to the middle of the plot
                     label = "p.signif", label.x.npc = "center", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("< 72 hours" = "#acdaa6", 
                               "72 or more hours" = "#387830")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("<72 Hours", "≥72 Hours")) +
  # Adding padding y axis 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.1, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Categorized Hours") +
  labs(title= "MCDI: Overall Language\nTwo-Group Comparison",
       subtitle= "(p = 0.015)") 

figure4_mcdi

#jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/raw/figure4_mcdi.jpeg",
#    width = 1600, height = 2800)
#figure4_mcdi
#invisible(dev.off())




#scatter
figure4_mcdi_scat <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_wrist_mcdi, aes(x=hours, y=value)) + 
  geom_point(size= 1.5) + 
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=F, size=1.5, color="#387830", linetype="dashed") +
  scale_shape_manual(values= c(16)) +
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(0.7,0.75), size=10, face="bold") + 
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.1, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Total Daytime Hours") + 
  labs(title= "MCDI: Overall Language\nCorrelation",
    subtitle= "(rho = 0.26, p = 0.010)")

figure4_mcdi_scat


#jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/raw/figure4_mcdi_scat.jpeg",
#    width = 2000, height = 2000)
#figure4_mcdi_scat
#invisible(dev.off())



#combine the FOUR plots to make final figure 
figure4 <-  figure4_mcdi + figure4_pas + figure4_mcdi_scat + figure4_pas_scat + 
  plot_layout(ncol=2, heights = c(2, 1)) + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a", tag_prefix = "(", tag_suffix = ")") &
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", ))


figure4

jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/figure4.jpeg",
    width = 9, height = 9, units="in", res=1200)
figure4
invisible(dev.off())



```

FIGURE 5
select attributes for ankle band
boxplots with categorized hours (x) by attribute scale (y) and wilcox test
scatter plots with total daytime hours (x) and attribute scale (y) and spearman correlation
attributes included: PAS and MCDI
```{r}

#figure 4 individual plots (ankle)

#set up 
long_wk_child_day_ankle_biq_nov <- long_wk_child_day %>% 
  filter(attribute== "BIQ_Nov_T30", 
         position== "ankle")

long_wk_child_day_ankle_biq_soc <- long_wk_child_day %>% 
  filter(attribute== "BIQ_Soc_T30", 
         position== "ankle")

long_wk_child_day_ankle_biq_tot <- long_wk_child_day %>% 
  filter(attribute== "BIQ_Tot_T30", 
         position== "ankle")

long_wk_child_day_ankle_ecbq_effco <- long_wk_child_day %>% 
  filter(attribute== "ECBQ_EFFCO_T30", 
         position== "ankle")


## FIRST COLUMN ##

#ankle: biq novelty 
figure5_biq_nov <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_ankle_biq_nov, aes(x=cat_hours, y=value, fill=cat_hours)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, lwd=1, alpha=1, position= position_dodge(width=5)) +
  geom_jitter(size=1.5, position = position_jitterdodge(0.3)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                     # Changing the value to asterisks and moving to the middle of the plot
                     label = "p.signif", label.x.npc = "center", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("< 72 hours" = "#acdaa6", 
                               "72 or more hours" = "#387830")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("<72 Hours", "≥72 Hours")) +
  # Adding padding y axis 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.1, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Categorized Hours") +
  labs(title= "BIQ: Novelty\nTwo-Group Comparison",
       subtitle= "(p = 0.003)")
  
  
figure5_biq_nov


#scatter
figure5_biq_nov_scat <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_ankle_biq_nov, aes(x=hours, y=value)) + 
  geom_point(size= 1.5) + 
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=F, size=1.5, color="#387830", linetype="dashed") +
  scale_shape_manual(values= c(16)) +
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(0.7,0.75), size=10, face="bold") + 
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.1, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Total Daytime Hours") + 
  labs(title= "BIQ: Novelty\nCorrelation",
    subtitle= "(rho = 0.31, p = 0.002)")

figure5_biq_nov_scat













## COLUMN 2 ##

#ankle: biq social 
figure5_biq_soc <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_ankle_biq_soc, aes(x=cat_hours, y=value, fill=cat_hours)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, lwd=1, alpha=1, position= position_dodge(width=5)) +
  geom_jitter(size=1.5, position = position_jitterdodge(0.3)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                     # Changing the value to asterisks and moving to the middle of the plot
                     label = "p.signif", label.x.npc = "center", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("< 72 hours" = "#f8c198", 
                               "72 or more hours" = "#e26f1a")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("<72 Hours", "≥72 Hours")) +
  # Adding padding y axis 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.1, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Categorized Hours") +
  labs(title= "BIQ: Social\nTwo-Group Comparison",
       subtitle= "(p = 0.001)")

figure5_biq_soc



figure5_biq_soc_scat <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_ankle_biq_soc, aes(x=hours, y=value)) + 
  geom_point(size= 1.5) + 
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=F, size=1.5, color="#e26f1a", linetype="dashed") +
  scale_shape_manual(values= c(16)) +
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(0.7,0.75), size=10, face="bold") + 
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.1, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Total Daytime Hours") + 
  labs(title= "BIQ: Social\nCorrelation",
    subtitle= "(rho = 0.35, p < 0.001)")
  
  
  
figure5_biq_soc_scat











# COLUMN 3 ##


#ankle: biq total 
figure5_biq_tot <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_ankle_biq_tot, aes(x=cat_hours, y=value, fill=cat_hours)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, lwd=1, alpha=1, position= position_dodge(width=5)) +
  geom_jitter(size=1.5, position = position_jitterdodge(0.3)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                     # Changing the value to asterisks and moving to the middle of the plot
                     label = "p.signif", label.x.npc = "center", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("< 72 hours" = "#dbabf0", 
                               "72 or more hours" = "#af69cd")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("<72 Hours", "≥72 Hours")) +
  # Adding padding y axis 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.1, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Categorized Hours") +
  labs(title= "BIQ: Total\nTwo-Group Comparison",
       subtitle= "(p = 0.001)")
  
figure5_biq_tot



#scatter
figure5_biq_tot_scat <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_ankle_biq_tot, aes(x=hours, y=value)) + 
  geom_point(size= 1.5) + 
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=F, size=1.5, color="#af69cd", linetype="dashed") +
  scale_shape_manual(values= c(16)) +
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(0.7,0.75), size=10, face="bold") + 
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.1, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Total Daytime Hours") + 
  labs(title= "BIQ: Total\nCorrelation",
    subtitle= "(rho = 0.34, p < 0.001)")

figure5_biq_tot_scat




# COLUMN 4 ##

#ankle: ecbq effco
figure5_ecbq_effco <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_ankle_ecbq_effco, aes(x=cat_hours, y=value, fill=cat_hours)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, lwd=1, alpha=1, position= position_dodge(width=5)) +
  geom_jitter(size=1.5, position = position_jitterdodge(0.3)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                     # Changing the value to asterisks and moving to the middle of the plot
                     label = "p.signif", label.x.npc = "center", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("< 72 hours" = "#e997ae", 
                               "72 or more hours" = "#c84268")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("<72 Hours", "≥72 Hours")) +
  # Adding padding y axis 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.3, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Categorized Hours") +
  labs(title= "ECBQ: Effortful Control\nTwo-Group Comparison",
       subtitle= "(p = 0.016)")
  
figure5_ecbq_effco





figure5_ecbq_effco_scat <- ggplot(long_wk_child_day_ankle_ecbq_effco, aes(x=hours, y=value)) + 
  geom_point(size= 1.5) + 
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=F, size=1.5, color="#c84268", linetype="dashed") +
  scale_shape_manual(values= c(16)) +
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(0.7,0.75), size=10, face="bold") + 
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.3, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Attribute Scale") +
  xlab("Total Daytime Hours") + 
  labs(title= "ECBQ: Effortful Control\nCorrelation",
    subtitle= "(rho = -0.2, p = 0.041)")


figure5_ecbq_effco_scat



#figures combined

#combine the FOUR plots to make final figure 
figure5 <-  figure5_biq_nov + figure5_biq_soc + figure5_biq_tot + figure5_ecbq_effco + figure5_biq_nov_scat + figure5_biq_soc_scat + figure5_biq_tot_scat + figure5_ecbq_effco_scat + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a", tag_prefix = "(", tag_suffix = ")") +
  plot_layout(ncol=4, heights = c(2, 1)) &
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"))
  
figure5

jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/figure5.jpeg",
    width = 16, height = 9, units="in", res=1200)
figure5
invisible(dev.off())


```



boxplot of age (supp figure 1)
```{r}

# wrist boxplot with wear time category (x) by age (y)
figure_age_wrist <- ggplot(demo_weartime_wrist, aes(x=cat6day, y=age, fill=cat6day)) + 
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, lwd=1, alpha=1) +
  geom_jitter(size=1.5, position = position_jitterdodge(0.2)) +
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",
                     # Changing the value to asterisks and moving to the middle of the plot
                     label = "p.signif", label.x.npc = "center", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("< 72 hours" = "#A6CAED", 
                               "72 or more hours" = "#6699CC")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("<72 Hours", "≥72 Hours")) +
  # Adding padding y axis 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.3, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Child Age (months)") +
  xlab("Categorized Hours")
  

figure_age_wrist

wilcox_age_wrist <- wilcox.test(age ~ cat6day, data= demo_weartime_wrist)
formatted_p_value <- format.pval(wilcox_age_wrist$p.value, digits = 3)
print(formatted_p_value)

# ankle boxplot with wear time category (x) by age (y)

figure_age_ankle <- ggplot(demo_weartime_ankle, aes(x=cat6day, y=age, fill=cat6day)) + 
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, lwd=1, alpha=1) +
  geom_jitter(size=1.5, position = position_jitterdodge(0.2)) +
  #stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",                                                       ## removed to not have the "ns" showing
                     # Changing the value to asterisks and moving to the middle of the plot
                     #label = "p.signif", label.x.npc = "center", size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("< 72 hours" = "#acdaa6", 
                               "72 or more hours" = "#387830")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("<72 Hours", "≥72 Hours")) +
  # Adding padding y axis 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14, color = "black"), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 7, b=0, l=0)),
        
        axis.line.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.line.y=element_blank(),
        
        axis.title=element_text(size=16, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-3), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 10, b=0, l=0)),
        
        title=element_text(size=14),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=5, hjust=0.5, face="bold"),
        #plot.title.position="plot",
        subtitle=element_markdown(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(vjust=1, hjust=1, size=14), 
        
        plot.margin= unit(c(0.3, 0.3, 0.3, 0.3), "inches"), 
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=1.5),
        legend.position= "none") +
  ylab("Child Age (months)") +
  xlab("Categorized Hours")
  

figure_age_ankle


supp_figure1 <-  figure_age_wrist + figure_age_ankle + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"))

supp_figure1

jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/supp_figure1.jpeg",
    width = 10, height = 5, units="in", res=1200)
supp_figure1
invisible(dev.off())

```




cleaned figure, not in manuscript
scatter plots of female and male wrist (x) by ankle (y) weartime 
```{r}

#individual plots for figure 

# female
scat_f <- ggplot(demo_weartime_female, aes(x=wrist_hours, y=ankle_hours, shape=sex)) + 
  geom_point(size= 24) + 
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=F, size=7)+
  scale_shape_manual(values= c(15)) +
  #labs(title= "Figure 2") +
  ylab("Ankle Daytime Hours") +
  xlab("Wrist Daytime Hours") + 
  labs(title= "(A) Female") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(30,35), size=40, fontface="bold") + 
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=100, face="bold", color = "black"), 
        axis.title=element_text(size=110, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-1.5), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 50, b=0, l=0)),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 30, b=0, l=0)),
        axis.line=element_line((size=10), color="black"),
        title=element_text(size=100, face="bold"),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=2), 
        legend.title=element_text(size=100),
        legend.text=element_text(size=100), 
        plot.margin= unit(c(1.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1.5), "inches"), 
        legend.position= "none",
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=10))

scat_f

#jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/raw/scat_f.jpeg",
#    width = 2400, height = 2400)
#scat_f
#invisible(dev.off())



# male
scat_m <- ggplot(demo_weartime_male, aes(x=wrist_hours, y=ankle_hours, shape=sex)) + 
  geom_point(size= 24) + 
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=F, size=7)+
  scale_shape_manual(values= c(16)) +
  #labs(title= "Figure 2") +
  ylab("Ankle Daytime Hours") +
  xlab("Wrist Daytime Hours") + 
  labs(title= "(B) Male") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x=c(3,5), label.y=c(30,35), size=40, face="bold") + 
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=100, face="bold", color = "black"), 
        axis.title=element_text(size=110, face="bold", color = "black"),
        axis.text.x=element_text(vjust=-1),
        axis.title.x=element_text(vjust=-1.5), 
        axis.title.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 50, b=0, l=0)),
        axis.text.y=element_text(margin= ggplot2::margin(t=0, r= 30, b=0, l=0)),
        axis.line=element_line((size=10), color="black"),
        title=element_text(size=100, face="bold"),
        plot.title=element_text(vjust=2), 
        legend.title=element_text(size=100),
        legend.text=element_text(size=100), 
        plot.margin= unit(c(1.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1.5), "inches"), 
        legend.position= "none",
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=10))

scat_m

#jpeg("2_OutputGraphs/raw/scat_m.jpeg",
#    width = 2400, height = 2400)
#scat_m
#invisible(dev.off())

#combine the two plots to make final figure 
scatter_bysex <- scat_f + scat_m +
  #plot_layout(widths = c(3, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"))
  
scatter_bysex


```



package citations to add to endnote for the manuscript
```{r}

citation("openxlsx") # for reading and writing out files
citation("table1") # for making tables
citation("tidyverse") # for data organization and manipulation
citation("ggpubr") 
citation("rstatix")
citation("ltm") # cronbach's alpha
citation("FSA") # dunns test
citation("patchwork")

```
